2% of jet fuel must be sustainable as of: The European Parliament voted to mandate that 2% of jet fuel should be sustainable by 2025, increasing to 70% by 2050. Hydrogen and fuel derived from recycled cooking oil are recognized as green options for aviation fuel, with an EU eco-label for flights set to launch in 2025.
All MEPs to publish meetings with repres: MEPs voted to disclose meetings with interest groups and third countries, and to declare assets at the start and end of their term. Tighter rules were also approved for conflicts of interest, second income, and gifts, while unofficial groupings' activities were regulated and restricted.
The European Parliament adopted by 538 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution strengthening EU-Switzerland relations, emphasizing their shared values and economic partnership. The resolution underscores the EU's desire to collaborate with Switzerland on various issues.
A worker will be exposed to levels of as: Workers' asbestos exposure limits have been reduced significantly, with the implementation of more advanced technology for detection. New measures aim to enhance worker protection and minimize asbestos exposure.
Member states obliged to protect media a: Media protection against interference was mandated for member states. Use of spyware against journalists was prohibited, and all media were required to disclose their ownership, with fair state advertising distribution.
The European Parliament adopted by 519 v: The European Parliament passed an amendment (519 votes in favor, 99 against, 8 abstentions) to improve chemical safety regulations, focusing on human health and environmental protection, and promoting alternative methods for substance assessment. The amendment also includes consideration of gender differences in chemical susceptibility.
First standard in the world to define gr: The European Parliament passed a resolution, establishing the first global standard for green bonds, requiring issuers to present green transition plans. The rules also govern external reviewers of European green bonds.
The European Parliament adopted by 519 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by a large majority, condemning the escalating human rights abuses in Afghanistan, particularly the persecution of former government officials and the oppression of women and girls. The resolution was tabled by multiple political groups.
The European Parliament adopted by 448 v: The European Parliament, by a significant majority, approved a resolution endorsing Moldova's path towards EU membership, emphasizing its commitment to eventual EU membership and urging the Commission and European Council to initiate accession talks by the end of 2023, with a focus on continuing Moldova's reform process to improve living standards.
The European Parliament adopted by 568 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution to transform the Farm Accountancy Data Network into a Farm Sustainability Data Network, aiming to assess the impact on agriculture under the Common Agricultural Policy (CAP).
The European Parliament adopted by 305 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 305 votes in favor, 129 against, and 69 abstentions, to develop a strategy for addressing Europe's protein shortage in food and feed production, aiming to reduce dependence on imports from non-EU countries.
The European Parliament adopted by 447 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution aimed at farm renewal in the EU, addressing an aging farmer population. Currently, over half (57.6%) of farm managers are 55 or older, while only 12% are under 40. The issue is most severe in states with a high proportion of elderly farmers and few young ones. As farming is largely family-based, this trend may impact future operations.
The European Parliament adopted by 544 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to update the 2023 EU budget, passing it with 544 votes in favor, 24 against, and 41 abstentions. The budget includes financing for the new Defence Industrial Reinforcement Instrument and the European Chips Act, with increased revenue forecasts for customs duties, VAT base, and non-recycled plastic packaging waste.
New Ukraine Facility with overall capaci: The European Parliament approved a €50 billion New Ukraine Facility (2024-2027), enhancing its transparency and democratic accountability. The decision includes the possibility of using frozen Russian assets for Ukraine's reconstruction.
Urgent need for political stability in t: Parliament approved measures to strengthen political stability and EU integration in the country, addressing concerns over potential malign foreign interference.
The European Parliament adopted by 424 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, passing by a majority of 424 votes, to increase funding for programs addressing Ukraine war consequences and high energy prices. The focus is on research, innovation, Erasmus+ scholarships, Eastern Neighbourhood aid, and agricultural support for young farmers.
Limited progress in bringing those respo: Parliament condemns insufficient accountability for high-level suspects, emphasizing the need to combat impunity, safeguard media independence, and uphold pluralism. The vote indicates a call for improved law enforcement and justice systems.
The European Parliament adopted by 522 v: The European Parliament passed a recommendation (522 votes for, 27 against, 53 abstentions) to bolster electoral rights in illiberal political systems and authoritarian regimes, addressing concerns over democratic decline in established democracies.
The European Parliament adopted by 595 v: The European Parliament approved amendments to the waste electrical and electronic equipment (WEEE) directive, with 595 votes in favor, 2 against, and 3 abstentions. The changes will be further negotiated in interinstitutional discussions. The amendments aim to address the Court of Justice of the European Union's judgment in case C-181/20, focusing on ensuring legal certainty in future revisions of the WEEE directive.
Cleaner road vehicles and improved air q: Parliament approved stricter emissions limits for buses and heavy-duty vehicles, aiming for cleaner road vehicles and improved air quality, aligning with international standards.
The European Parliament adopted by 499 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (499 votes in favor, 56 against, 52 abstentions) to boost visibility for the European Solidarity Corps program 2021-2027. Recognizing the need for increased awareness among youth organizations, particularly at regional and local levels, they aim to leverage the European Year of Youth in 2022 to enhance the program's visibility and establish a recognizable brand. The Commission and national agencies are encouraged to collaborate more closely on best practices, procedures, and promotion efforts.
Trade deal sets out unique sanctionable: The European Parliament approved a trade deal that includes enforceable commitments on climate and labor standards, as well as protection for EU geographical indications. This agreement offers EU companies equal access to the procurement market.
The European Parliament adopted by 616 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 616 votes in favor and 5 against, aiming to enhance audit reliability in shared EU fund management. The resolution highlighted the challenges of complex EU fund rules and frequent regulatory changes, leading to potential compliance issues and implementation gaps, increasing error risk. Members expressed concern over excessive bureaucracy and varying implementation standards by Member States, potentially negatively affecting the attractiveness of EU funds.
The European Parliament has adopted, by: The European Parliament passed a resolution on job creation through a green transition, focusing on renewable energies, energy efficiency, and the circular economy. This transition is projected to create up to 2 million jobs in the short and medium term, with potential for an additional 10 million as it progresses. Each EUR 1 million investment in building energy renovation could generate approximately 18 EU jobs.
The European Parliament adopted by 430 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (430-172) affirming the principle of EU law's supremacy over national laws, emphasizing its importance for consistent application and equal treatment of EU citizens.
The European Parliament adopted by 555 v: The European Parliament passed a directive aimed at digitizing judicial cooperation, with 555 votes in favor, 5 against, and 4 abstentions. This decision was made during the first reading under the ordinary legislative procedure.
The European Parliament adopted by 551 v: The European Parliament approved a regulation on digitalizing judicial cooperation, aiming to unify electronic communication in cross-border civil, commercial, and criminal cases. The vote was 551 in favor, 5 against, with 6 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 557 v: The European Parliament unanimously passed a regulation to establish a high common level of cybersecurity across Union institutions, with 27 abstentions. The regulation outlines measures for internal cybersecurity risk management, reporting, and information sharing within Union entities.
The European Parliament adopted, by 433: The European Parliament passed a resolution (433-100) to improve social and professional conditions for artists and workers in the Cultural and Creative Sectors (CCS). Recognizing disparities across Europe, they aim to address varying social security coverage and enhance living and working conditions. The cultural sector, employing 3.8% of the workforce and contributing 4.2% to GDP, requires better protection.
Certification framework to ensure high q: The European Parliament voted to establish a certification framework for high-quality carbon removals, aiming to prevent greenwashing. The vote passed, focusing on distinguishing carbon removals, carbon farming, and carbon storage in products.
The European Parliament adopted by 386 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution aimed at reducing inequalities and promoting social inclusion for children and their families during crises, with 386 votes in favor, 87 against, and 141 abstentions. The resolution targets low-income and lower-education families, single-parent families, families from disadvantaged regions, ethnic minorities, and families with disabilities to combat poverty and social exclusion.
Support for 421 former workers of the re: The European Parliament approved a support package for 421 former Makro Cash & Carry workers in the Wallonia region, following the retail chain's bankruptcy in January 2023. The package includes advisory services, job search assistance, and skills training.
Improving EU air quality, in line with G: The European Parliament voted to require zero-emission urban buses by 2030, aiming to improve air quality and align with Green Deal and REPowerEU objectives. The decision targets heavy-duty vehicles as a significant contributor to EU road transport emissions.
The European Parliament adopted by 401 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (401-39, 109 abstentions) to support harnessing talent in Europe's regions. They endorsed the Commission communication 'Harnessing talent in Europe’s regions', aiming to promote and attract talent across all regions. Members advocated for additional budget resources for cohesion policy to address future challenges, emphasizing the importance of 'rural proofing' for demographic impact, service accessibility, and rural development.
The European Parliament adopted by 291 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution to amend EU treaties, aiming to enhance the Union's ability to act, democratic legitimacy, and accountability, addressing geopolitical challenges more effectively. The current institutional framework is insufficient for a 27-member Union, necessitating treaty revisions due to future enlargements.
Less unnecessary packaging and waste\nBa: The European Parliament voted to ban "forever chemicals" in food packaging, aiming to reduce unnecessary packaging waste.
The European Parliament adopted by 495 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (495-27) to strengthen access to education in developing countries, emphasizing free, inclusive, and compulsory basic education, and equal access to technical, professional, and higher education. The parliament also highlighted education's role in promoting shared values, raising awareness, and reducing conflict.
The European Parliament adopted by 465 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 465 votes in favor, to create harmonized definitions for start-ups and scale-ups, aiming to boost innovation and competitiveness. The definitions will focus on scalability and distinguish these entities from SMEs during fundraising, growth, and maturity phases.
The European Parliament adopted by 548 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (548 votes in favor, 33 against, 18 abstentions) to restructure EU structural funds for regions impacted by automotive, green, and digital transitions. The Parliament emphasized that cohesion policy should not be used as a budgetary compensator or suffer cuts during crises, and it should serve as a long-term investment strategy to help regions adapt to various industrial, technological, ecological, energy, digital, social, and demographic changes. Members acknowledged the challenges posed by green and digital transitions to economic, social, and territorial cohesion.
Parliament asks for a binding deal with: The Parliament called for a binding agreement with the Council on the method of electing the Commission President. Measures were proposed to ensure voting rights for mobile citizens and those with disabilities, aiming to boost voter turnout across member states. A push was made for advancements in the latest electoral law reform and new rules for European political parties.
Current rules limit protection from hate: Parliament voted to expand protections against hate speech, rejecting current limits focused on specific motives like race, religion, or national origin. The decision was made in response to rising hate incidents, criticizing the Council's inaction regarding this issue.
The European Parliament adopted by 495 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (495-65) to further reform corporate taxation rules, emphasizing the need for harmonization to combat harmful tax competition, profit shifting, and tax avoidance/evasion. The Parliament acknowledged the Commission's proposal for tax simplification for SMEs and BEFIT, stressing the importance of EU-level solutions that are well-designed and consider existing Member State systems.
Call to ban addictive techniques like en: Vote Outcome: A call to limit addictive online practices such as endless scrolling and auto-play was passed, aiming to shift towards ethical design and introducing a digital "right to not be disturbed." This new regulation requires all online services and products to be child-safe.
Rules on geo-blocking have been in force: Vote Outcome: Geo-blocking rules in the audio-visual sector have been proposed to be extended, aiming to eliminate barriers for cross-border access to content like live sports events and audio-visual catalogues.
The European Parliament adopted by 482 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution on mental health, emphasizing its multifactorial nature influenced by socio-economic, environmental, biological, and genetic factors. A cross-sectoral approach was advocated to prevent and mitigate mental health impacts on individuals, communities, and societies.
The European Parliament adopted by 477 v: The European Parliament approved changes to rest periods for drivers in occasional passenger transport, allowing two breaks of at least 15 minutes each (instead of three) while maintaining a total minimum rest time of 45 minutes. Negotiations on the amendments will continue.
The European Parliament adopted by 357 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution to integrate sustainable development principles into EU external actions regarding extractive industries in developing countries. It emphasized the need for equal partnerships between the EU and these dependent nations, prioritizing mutually beneficial projects that focus on people and the environment.
The European Parliament adopted by 578 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution aimed at addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs), causing 90% of deaths and accounting for 80% of health burden in EU countries. The resolution focuses on reducing premature deaths from CVDs, cancers, diabetes, and chronic respiratory diseases, which account for 68% of all premature deaths in Europe, and aims to control costs estimated at EUR 115 billion annually.
The European Parliament adopted by 500 v: The European Parliament voted to reject a proposal for amending maximum residue levels of tricyclazole, a potentially harmful fungicide used in rice production, with 500 votes in favor, 42 against, and 4 abstentions. The use and import of products containing excessive tricyclazole residues have been banned since 2016.
The European Parliament adopted by 380 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 380 votes in favor and 156 against, objecting to the approval of genetically modified maize products. An application to market these products was submitted by Syngenta Crop Protection AG in Germany.
The European Parliament adopted by 371 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (371-92) outlining political guidelines for future reviews of the Common Fisheries Policy. The resolution addresses the implementation of current policies, questions relevance of objectives and tools, and suggests potential reforms focusing on fish stock conservation and fisheries management, including maximum sustainable yield, landing obligations, and quota issues.
The European Parliament adopted by 559 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (559-20, 36 abstentions) on cross-border cooperation with neighboring countries. This collaboration, spanning 33 nations, 184 regions, and 260 million people, is crucial to EU policies, promoting peace and sustainable development along EU borders. The Parliament emphasized the potential benefits of aligning Interreg NEXT programs with Interreg programs for simplified procedures, synergies, and increased effectiveness of European investments, but also stressed the need for more simplification.
Revenues from the EU music streaming mar: MEPs voted to advocate for transparent algorithms and recommendation tools in the EU music streaming market, aiming to ensure fair distribution of revenues for European musical works.
The European Parliament adopted by 437 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (437-158) to strengthen EU citizenship, emphasizing its role in fostering a shared democratic identity and offering specific rights, duties, and freedoms. However, they acknowledged that these benefits are not fully realized due to insufficient awareness among citizens.
The European Parliament adopted by 486 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (486-62, 61 abstentions) to implement the Erasmus+ program (2021-2027), a EU initiative supporting education, training, youth, and sport. The program emphasizes inclusion, green and digital transitions, and democratic participation by young people, with an estimated budget of €26.2 billion.
The European Parliament adopted by 366 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (366-41) to boost energy access in developing countries, recognizing it as crucial for achieving Sustainable Development Goals. The EU was urged to promote renewable energy in developing nations via policy, regulation, and administrative support.
The European Parliament adopted by 585 v: The European Parliament approved, with a large majority, a resolution strengthening EU-India relations. The resolution endorses a planned leaders' meeting in New Delhi in early 2024 to reaffirm commitments and review progress towards the 2025 EU-India Roadmap.
The European Parliament adopted by 531 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 531 votes in favor and 2 against, advocating for increased exploitation of geothermal energy due to its potential benefits for heating and cooling needs. The resolution highlighted challenges such as limited public awareness and high upfront investments, but also noted advancements in technology expanding the feasibility of cost-efficient geothermal projects.
The European Parliament adopted by 383 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (383-99) addressing the impact of the energy crisis and rising cost of living on women, highlighting the exacerbation of gender inequalities and threats to women's livelihoods, health, and economic independence.
The European Parliament adopted by 409 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution on European historical consciousness, emphasizing the importance of a responsible, evidence-based approach to history in fostering understanding and reconciliation.
Generic environmental claims and other m: Environmental misrepresentations are banned, allowing only sustainability labels from approved certifications or public authorities. A new transparent guarantee extension label is introduced.
The European Parliament adopted by 484 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (484-45, 98 abstentions) on the opportunities, risks, and policy implications of virtual worlds for the single market. The digitalization is seen as offering potential solutions to global issues like poverty, inequality, sustainability, and democratizing education, but also presents challenges that may necessitate a reevaluation of established economic and social norms while preserving core principles.
The European Parliament adopted by 415 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 415 votes in favor and 129 abstentions, to improve the implementation of the Common Market Organisation (CMO) Regulation in fisheries and aquaculture. The resolution emphasized the importance of CMO in achieving objectives of the Common Fisheries Policy, but noted low uptake in creating fish producer organizations due to complex setup, recognition issues, and financial/legal uncertainties.
The European Parliament adopted by 446 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 446 votes in favor and 48 against, focusing on combating fraud and protecting the EU's financial interests in 2022. Despite a slight increase in reported cases (12,455) and affected financing (EUR 1.77 billion), the resolution emphasized the need for civil society involvement to improve prevention and detection of fraud.
The European Parliament adopted by 521 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 521 votes in favor and 19 against, to amend the MiFID 2 directive, aiming to boost transparency in financial markets and enhance the competitiveness of the EU's capital markets. The resolution introduces the term 'systematic internaliser' for investment firms regularly trading on their own account.
The European Parliament adopted by 476 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (476-130) on implementing the Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values program 2021-2027, focusing on promoting civic engagement, social equality, gender equality, regional heritage preservation, and combating discrimination and violence.
The European Parliament adopted by 425 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (425-75) to improve the implementation of Regulation (EC) No 1924/2006 on nutrition and health claims for foods. This regulation aims to ensure consumer protection and facilitate informed choices, but misleading claims persist in food sales.
The European Parliament adopted by 492 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (492-73) emphasizing the expanding role in EU foreign and security policy, highlighting the added value of parliamentary diplomacy in complementing the EEAS, Commission, and Member States' diplomatic services. Members see potential for further enhancing Parliament's role.
The European Parliament adopted by 397 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (397 votes for, 134 against) emphasizing transparency and accountability for NGOs funded from the EU budget. The resolution highlights the importance of NGOs in representing civil society and upholding EU values, while advocating that only NGOs respecting these rights should receive EU funding.
The European Parliament adopted by 324 v: The European Parliament approved a directive to reduce debt-equity bias and limit interest tax deductions, with amendments to aid SMEs, passing by 324 votes to 132.
Key issues include media freedom, corrup: The European Parliament voted to uphold media freedom, combat corruption, and protect individual liberties, particularly for women, children, LGBTIQ+, and other vulnerable groups. Recommendations were made for Hungary, Poland, Greece, Cyprus, and Spain regarding human rights issues.
The European Parliament adopted by 386 v: The European Parliament rejected a proposal to extend the use of insecticide thiacloprid beyond its expiration date on February 3, 2021. Thiacloprid, an active ingredient in pesticides used on various crops, was not renewed due to unproven safety concerns.
Focus on consumer safety: robust and up-: The European Parliament voted to strengthen consumer safety by implementing advanced fraud detection and identity verification for instant payments. A cap on charges for instant transfers was also established, allowing clients to set a maximum limit for their transfers.
The European Parliament adopted by 628 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 628 votes in favor and 3 against, to amend the Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) Directive. The amendment emphasizes the importance of avoiding retroactive effects in future revisions, providing clarity for Electrical and Electronic Equipment (EEE) producers.
Rules for exposure to lead updated for t: Lead exposure rules updated after 40 years, and limits for diisocyanates established for the first time, enhancing worker safety during the green transition.
The European Parliament adopted by 630 v: The European Parliament, with 630 votes in favor and 6 abstentions, approved a resolution to align Council Decision 2009/917/JHA on data protection with Union rules. This aims to establish a unified framework for personal data protection in the Union, following the principles of the Directive on law enforcement data protection.
Easier procedure for NGT plants consider: The European Parliament voted to simplify the approval process for Next-Generation Technologies (NGT) plants, deemed equivalent to conventional ones. Mandatory labeling for all NGT products remains in place.
The European Parliament adopted by 392 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution supporting the EU's LGBTIQ Equality Strategy 2020-2025, expressing concern over disparities in implementation among member states and ongoing discrimination against LGBTIQ+ individuals.
Hostile environment for media and journa: Parliament calls for an assessment of EU funds' disbursement, addressing concerns over media hostility, police use of spyware, and corruption.
Secrecy of vote breached, group voting,: EU Parliament condemns alleged electoral misconduct and attacks on observers in Serbia, calling for a suspension of EU funding due to severe breaches of the rule of law.
The European Parliament adopted by 418 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on the European Central Bank's annual report 2023, with 418 votes in favor, 157 against, and 42 abstentions. The resolution reflects expectations of economic growth slowing from 3.5% in 2022 to 0.9% in 2023, before rebounding to 1.5% in 2024, while inflation is projected to average 5.6% in 2023, decreasing to 3.2% in 2024 and 2.1% in 2025. Government deficits are expected to decline to 3.1% of GDP in 2023 and 2.4% in 2024, with a decrease in the government debt to GDP ratio in the euro area and EU-27.
EU protection against abusive cross-bord: The European Parliament voted to approve measures aimed at protecting businesses from abusive cross-border lawsuits. This includes the ability to dismiss unfounded cases early and provide financial protection for defendants, while also limiting forum-shopping and enhancing access to information for victims.
Will prevent illegal use of geographical: The European Parliament voted to regulate the use of geographical indications online, ensuring that producer names and GIs appear together on packaging for processed products, and simplified the registration process for new GIs with a six-month time limit for assessing requests.
Democracy, the rule of law, and fundamen: The European Parliament voted on safeguarding democracy, rule of law, and fundamental rights across the EU. A concern was raised about threats to civil society, media, independent authorities, and vulnerable groups in several member states, particularly Spain, France, Greece, Slovakia, Malta, and Hungary. The outcome of the vote is not specified in the summary provided.
Make decision-making more effective by m:
Vote Outcome: Shift from unanimous decision-making towards treaty mechanisms to enhance EU effectiveness and budget flexibility.
Decision: Gradual integration of candidate countries into the single market, with roadmaps and timelines for each accession country.
Short-term rentals represent around 25%: The European Parliament voted to harmonize short-term rental regulations across the EU, aiming to reduce market fragmentation and combat illegal listings.
The European Parliament adopted by 377 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (377 votes for, 90 against, 68 abstentions) advocating for human rights and democracy globally, emphasizing the EU's role in promoting and protecting these values. It also criticized authoritarian regimes undermining multilateral institutions like the UN Human Rights Council, urging reform to strengthen their democratic commitment.
The European Parliament adopted by 350 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (350 votes for, 91 against, 96 abstentions) on the implementation of the common security and defence policy, expressing concern over the escalating global security threats due to Russia's illegal war against Ukraine. They emphasized the need for European and transatlantic unity and international cooperation to address the challenges posed by this complex situation.
The European Parliament adopted by 338 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (338-86) expressing concern over Russia's illegal war against Ukraine, supported by Belarus and Iran. They called for an immediate end to military action, withdrawal of Russian troops from Ukraine and occupied territories.
Self-assessment of fitness to drive\nMin: Novice drivers' probationary period extended to a minimum of two years. Digital driving licences now allowed on smartphones.
Navalny’s murder yet another sign of the: The European Parliament calls for accountability for Navalny's murder, condemning it as a sign of Russia's repressive regime. They urge holding Putin accountable and distinguish between the Russian people and the Kremlin's authoritarian, militaristic, and corrupt regime, suggesting potential prisoner exchanges among member states.
There should be no self-imposed restrict: The European Parliament voted to remove self-imposed restrictions on providing military aid to Ukraine, and urged EU and NATO allies to support Ukraine with at least 0.25% of their GDP annually. They also called for immediate adoption of the U.S. military aid package for Ukraine by the House of Representatives.
An EU wallet to authenticate and access: Vote Outcome: EU approves development of a voluntary digital wallet for accessing services, e-signing documents, and managing privacy settings. Includes a privacy dashboard for user data control.
The European Parliament adopted by 420 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution for the 2024 European Semester, aiming to coordinate economic policies. Despite high inflation and tighter financing conditions, growth was estimated at 0.5% in both the EU and euro area in 2023, with projections revised down to 0.9% and 0.8% for 2024 respectively. The labor market remained strong, but inflation is projected to decrease from 6.3% in 2023 to 3.0% in 2024 and further to 2.5% in 2025.
The European Parliament adopted by 482 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, 482-120, to amend rules on breaks and rest periods for drivers in the occasional passenger transport sector, aiming to enhance fair competition, work conditions, and road safety.
New buildings to be zero-emission from 2: Buildings in the EU must become zero-emission by 2030, with measures aimed at reducing energy costs and combating climate change. Exceptions are made for agricultural and historical buildings.
The European Parliament adopted by 596 v: The European Parliament approved a regulation to enhance readability and management of labels for EU fertiliser products, passing with 596 votes in favor, 7 against, and 5 abstentions. This move aims to streamline the internal market by improving labeling for fertilisers.
Safeguards on general purpose artificial:
Parliament approved restrictions on AI use, including:
- Limiting biometric systems by law enforcement
- Banning social scoring and user manipulation
- Ensuring consumer rights for complaints and explanations.
Easier access to compensation for damage: Vote Outcome: Easier access to compensation for damage caused by defective products, including data loss and psychological harm, was approved, requiring businesses based in the EU to be held accountable.
Violating EU sanctions, including those: The European Parliament voted to make violations of EU sanctions, including those against Russia, criminal offenses, with penalties intended to deter such actions. However, the enforcement of these sanctions among member states was deemed uneven.
The European Parliament adopted by 568 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (568-22, 12 abstentions) to strengthen the independence of European Statistical agencies. The amended regulation emphasizes the need for objective and impartial statistics to build public trust in decisions based on them.
The European Parliament adopted by 580 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 580 votes in favor and 32 abstentions, to amend financial reporting requirements for entities active in financial markets. The amendments aim to enhance risk monitoring and oversight of the financial system.
The European Parliament adopted by 433 v: The European Parliament approved, with 433 votes in favor and 36 against, a resolution on the implementation and outcomes of the 2014-2020 Cohesion Policy. The resolution highlighted the positive impact of the policy, particularly in areas such as research and innovation, where it fostered collaboration between the research community and businesses, and aided in the commercialization of research outcomes. By 2022, over 75,000 companies had collaborated with research institutions, and around 37,000 had benefited from the policy.
The European Parliament adopted by 598 v: The European Parliament passed a Directive on asset recovery and confiscation, aimed at combating organized crime, terrorism, human trafficking, and drug trafficking. The Directive establishes minimum rules for tracing, freezing, and confiscating assets in criminal proceedings.
Strictest achievable emissions levels wi: The European Parliament voted to make stricter emissions levels mandatory for covered sectors, with the aim of reducing air, water, and soil pollution. A new EU Industrial Emissions Portal will improve public access to permits and information on local polluting activities. In 2026, the Commission will review whether cattle farms should be included.
The European Parliament adopted by 570 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (570 votes in favor, 20 against, 14 abstentions) to update the rules for the European Maritime Safety Agency. The amended proposal now requires the Agency to collaborate with the Commission and Member States, and to share research findings after Commission approval.
Road vehicles to stay cleaner for longer: Parliament approved stricter emission standards for buses and trucks, aiming to prolong vehicle cleanliness and enhance air quality. New regulations were also established to reduce particle emissions from tires and brakes, as well as boost battery longevity. An Environmental Vehicle Passport was introduced to include data on vehicles' environmental performance.
More robust cybersecurity for all produc: The European Parliament voted to strengthen cybersecurity for all digital products, including everyday items like smart doorbells, baby monitors, and Wi-Fi routers. Automatic security updates will be implemented when technically possible.
The European Parliament adopted by 464 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (464-92) establishing a common framework for media services within the EU (European Media Freedom Act). It aims to ensure access to diverse, editorially independent media content in all member states.
The European Parliament adopted by 517 v: The European Parliament approved, by a significant majority, a resolution to support ratification of the International Labour Organization's Violence and Harassment Convention (C190), aiming to establish a violence-free and harassment-free work environment globally. The Parliament granted consent to the draft Council decision.
More ambitious 2030 targets to reduce fo: The European Parliament voted to increase 2030 food waste reduction targets and to hold producers more accountable for textile products, clothing, and footwear. The proposal aims to decrease annual EU food and textile waste by 60 million and 12.6 million tonnes, respectively.
The European Parliament adopted by 504 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution aiming to strengthen EU-Armenia ties and promote a peace agreement with Azerbaijan, by 504 votes to 4, with 32 abstentions. The resolution emphasizes Armenia's economic reliance on Russia, particularly in the energy sector, and addresses increased exports from Armenia to Russia following EU sanctions.
The European Parliament adopted by 425 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 425 votes in favor and 64 against, to establish a common framework for decarbonizing natural gas and hydrogen markets, promoting the use of renewable and low-carbon gases and hydrogen, and facilitating a shift away from fossil gas.
Country of origin of honey must be clear: The European Parliament voted to require clear labeling of honey's country of origin and to develop a honey traceability system. Additionally, clearer labeling on sugar content in fruit juices and new rules for fruit in jams and marmalades were approved.
The European Parliament adopted by 479 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (479-116) establishing standards for independence of equality bodies promoting gender equality in employment. It also removed certain articles from previous directives.
The European Parliament adopted by 447 v: The European Parliament passed a regulation to boost renewable and natural gases, including hydrogen, in the energy system, aiming to shift away from fossil gas and meet the Union's 2030 climate goals and aim for climate neutrality by 2050.
A crisis entails mass arrivals of migran: The Parliament voted to approve rules addressing mass migration influxes, including those exploited by third countries or non-state actors to disrupt the EU, potentially altering standard asylum procedures.
The European Parliament decided to grant: The European Parliament approved the discharge of the European Food Safety Authority's Executive Director for the year 2022, and endorsed the closure of the accounts for that year, following the Court of Auditors' assurance of the accounts' reliability and legality.

The European Parliament approved the discharge of the European Medicines Agency's Executive Director for the 2022 financial year and endorsed the closure of the accounts, following the Court of Auditors' assurance of the accounts' reliability and legality. The decision was passed by 528 votes, with 71 votes against and 4 abstentions.
The European Parliament decided by 242 v: The European Parliament approved, by a majority of 242 votes, the use of €600 million from deferred projects in the 10th and 11th European Development Funds for addressing food security and economic crises in Africa, Caribbean, and other regions, in line with the Council's decision EU 2022/1223.
The European Parliament decided to grant: The European Parliament approved the discharge to the Director of the European Monitoring Centre for Drugs and Drug Addiction (EMCDDA) for the financial year 2022, and endorsed the closure of the accounts for that year, following the Court of Auditors' assurance of the reliability and legality of the accounts.

The European Parliament approved the discharge of the Director of the European Union Agency for Fundamental Rights for the financial year 2022, and endorsed the closure of the accounts, following the Court of Auditors' assurance of the accounts' reliability and legality. The decision was passed with 470 votes in favor, 123 against, and 3 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 585 v: The European Parliament approved, with a significant majority, a resolution to consent to the Voluntary Partnership Agreement with Côte d'Ivoire aimed at combating illegal timber trade, due to high deforestation rates in the country.
Novel EU rules on resettlement\nWill hel: The European Parliament voted to approve new rules for refugee resettlement, aiming to ensure safe passage and legal status for vulnerable refugees, while streamlining EU contributions to global resettlement efforts, with member states' pledges remaining voluntary.
Measures in place, set to expire next Au: The measures currently in effect, due to expire in August 2026, have been voted to be extended until 3 April 2026, emphasizing the need for a permanent framework agreement.
Equal access to preferential conditions: The European Parliament voted to provide equal access to preferential conditions for individuals with disabilities, including priority access and reserved parking. The European Disability Card will now be issued and renewed free of charge, extending to those temporarily residing in other EU countries for study purposes.
New rules to correct false self-employme: Vote Outcome: New rules to prevent false self-employment passed. Algorithmic decisions regarding worker dismissals are now prohibited. Personal data processing of certain types is forbidden for platforms.
The European Parliament adopted by 311 v: The European Parliament approved a regulation amending border rules for migrant movement, passing with 311 votes and 267 against, with 53 abstentions. The adopted text allows Member States to secure borders when large, forced groups attempt unauthorized entry, temporarily addressing instrumentalization of migrants.
The European Parliament adopted by 571 v: The European Parliament, in a vote of 571 for, 18 against, and 17 abstentions, approved a resolution to enhance competent authorities' access to cross-border bank account registries. The proposal aims to connect these registries through a single access point.
New provisions to foster investment, fac: The European Parliament voted to approve new rules aimed at boosting investment, promoting social convergence, and strengthening national commitments to plans. The rules establish minimum targets for deficit and debt reduction, with flexibility to extend timelines and allow exceptions in extraordinary circumstances. Initial national plans detailing expenditure, reforms, and investments are due by September 2024.
The European Parliament adopted by 604 v: The European Parliament, in a vote of 604 to 2 with 6 abstentions, approved a regulation on the transfer of criminal proceedings between EU member states, aiming to enhance justice administration within the common area of freedom, security, and justice. The approved version amends the initial proposal, applying to all criminal proceedings transfers within the Union.
The European Parliament adopted by 533 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 533 votes in favor and 31 against, to enhance public capital markets in the Union by amending Regulations (EU) 2017/1129, (EU) No 596/2014, and (EU) No 600/2014. The changes aim to make markets more appealing for companies and improve access to capital for small and medium-sized enterprises, requiring prior publication of a prospectus in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2017/1129.

The European Parliament passed, with few exceptions, a resolution to amend Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 on chemical substances and mixtures. The amendment recognizes that certain plant-derived substances may exhibit hazardous properties not apparent in their whole form, requiring additional evaluation.
Stricter 2030 limits for several air pol: Stricter air pollutant limits for 2030 were approved, aiming to standardize air quality indices across EU states and enhance citizens' access to justice and right to compensation for pollution-related harm.
The European Parliament adopted by 408 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, 408-63 with 153 abstentions, establishing a Single Market emergency instrument to address crises impacting the internal market. The proposal aims to safeguard free movement of goods, services, and people, and ensure the availability of essential goods.
The European Parliament adopted by 459 v: The European Parliament passed, with 459 votes in favor, a resolution to establish a common framework for producing high-quality EU labor market statistics on businesses. This replaces previous regulations from 1999 and 2003. The resolution covers various aspects of the business labor market.
The European Parliament adopted by 475 v: The European Parliament passed a directive establishing a framework for recovering and resolving insurance and reinsurance companies, aiming to enhance sector resilience and protect policyholders, taxpayers, economy, and financial stability.
The European Parliament adopted by 464 v: The European Parliament passed a regulation aimed at enhancing the transparency and integrity of Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) rating activities by 464 votes to 115, with 13 abstentions. The new regulation aims to improve the reliability and good governance of ESG ratings, contributing to the sustainable finance agenda of the Union.
The European Parliament adopted by 448 v: The European Parliament passed a directive aimed at managing concentration risk for central counterparties, focusing on Tier 2 entities providing systemically important services. The vote count was 448 in favor, 120 against, with 21 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 550 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (550 votes for, 3 against, 19 abstentions) to amend company law Directives, enhancing the use of digital tools and improving company data reliability in business registers. The goal is to facilitate cross-border branch and subsidiary setup using this data.
Job seeker’s support for 835 former work: Vote outcome: Support package for 835 job seekers from Vallourec Deutschland GmbH passed, providing upskilling, job search assistance, and business creation support following the company's decision to move production to Brazil.
The European Parliament adopted by 602 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 602 votes in favor and 3 against, simplifying reporting requirements for companies and public administrations, including the abolition of certain obligations under Directive 2014/62/EU on the protection of the euro.
The European Parliament adopted by 511 v: The European Parliament passed a regulation (511 votes in favor, 22 against, 75 abstentions) on payment services in the internal market, enhancing transparency for payment and electronic money services. The regulation sets uniform requirements for these services.
Products to last longer and be easier to: Parliament approved measures to extend product lifespan, promote repair, upgrade, and recycling. Unsold clothing and footwear destruction was prohibited.
Measures cover full life cycle of packag: The European Parliament voted to approve measures aimed at reducing packaging waste, including a ban on certain single-use plastic packaging types starting from January 1, 2030.
Revised environmental “conditionalities”: In a recent vote, the European Parliament approved changes allowing earlier use of conditional environmental support starting in 2024. Additionally, more exemptions from Common Agricultural Policy standards were permitted during extreme weather events, particularly for smaller farms.
MEPs have approved agreement between eig: MEPs approved an agreement to establish common ethical standards among EU institutions, marking a step towards a unified culture of integrity. Five independent experts will contribute to this effort.
The European Parliament adopted by 493 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (493-11, 18 abstentions) condemning Russia's undemocratic presidential elections in occupied territories, held amid repression and during their war against Ukraine. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups.
EU citizens must be protected from pesti: EU MEPs voted to protect citizens from pesticide residues in imported products, aiming for equal standards across all goods. The Commission is now required to reconsider its proposals accordingly.
Ukraine must have the possibility to def: Parliament calls for Ukraine's right to self-defense enhancement, condemns decreasing military aid from EU nations. MEPs urge stronger EU sanctions on Russia and its allies, emphasizing accountability for war crimes and reparations in any peace settlement.
Support from the EU Solidarity Fund for: The European Parliament approved aid for Italy, Slovenia, Austria, Greece, and France following 2023 floods. This assistance will help cover emergency and recovery costs. MEPs advocate for expanding EU aid due to rising instances of natural disasters in Europe.
Support for 365 former workers from reta: Parliament approved aid for 365 ex-Match-Smatch workers, providing advisory services, job search assistance, and skills training, following the retail chain's financial struggles and closures in Wallonia.
The European Parliament adopted by 444 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (444 votes for, 179 against, 20 abstentions) supporting updated employment guidelines. The focus is on education, new technologies, AI, and addressing issues like platform work, housing affordability, and labor shortages.
The European Parliament adopted by 621 v: The European Parliament, in a 621-10 vote (11 abstentions), approved a resolution to amend Directive 2006/112/EC on Value Added Tax (VAT), introducing an electronic exemption certificate for certain transactions. The proposal was adopted without amendments, aiming to simplify VAT processes.
Financial goal should be socially fair -: The European Parliament voted to advocate for socially fair financial goals, emphasizing that polluters should bear the costs. They also supported increasing green diplomacy efforts and expanding global emissions trading and carbon pricing.
The European Parliament adopted by 496 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 496 votes to 143, objecting to the authorization of genetically modified cotton COT102 in products. The decision was made on March 31, 2017, affecting Syngenta Crop Protection AG's application in Germany.
The European Parliament adopted by 468 v: The European Parliament rejected a decision to allow genetically modified maize MON 94804 in the market, with 468 votes in favor, 166 against, and 14 abstentions. The application to introduce this maize, submitted by Bayer Agriculture BV, was previously made to the Dutch authorities.
The European Parliament adopted by 464 v: The European Parliament rejected the renewal of the authorization for genetically modified maize MON 810 in animal feed, voting 464 to 163 with 27 abstentions. The decision overturns a previous authorization from 2017.
Support for 632 former workers from mach: Parliament approved a financial support package for 632 former workers from machinery and paper enterprises Purmo and Sappi in Limburg, primarily targeting lower-skilled workers aged 50 and over. The package includes counseling, vocational orientation, job search assistance, and skills training.
Need to resist efforts to roll back exis: Parliament voted to uphold existing rights and combat the feminization of poverty, supporting universal access to healthcare, including safe and legal abortions.
The European Parliament adopted by 441 v: The European Parliament approved a decision (441 votes for, 178 against) to establish a committee on the European Democracy Shield, aimed at addressing foreign interference in Union democracies. This move targets malicious actors using information manipulation and other tactics to influence EU democratic processes.
The European Parliament adopted by 480 v: The European Parliament approved the establishment of a special committee on the EU Housing Crisis, aiming to address the shortage of affordable homes affecting various income groups. This action aligns with the European Pillar of Social Rights, emphasizing the provision of quality housing for those in need at both Union and national levels.
The European Parliament adopted by 434 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 434 votes in favor and 30 against, condemning ongoing human rights violations in Azerbaijan, specifically targeting civil society, media, and the LGBTI+ community. The resolution highlights intensified repression around COP29, resulting in over 300 political prisoners and violations of prisoners' rights.
Parliament rejects Russia’s historical c: Parliament votes down Russia's historical justifications for an illegal war with Ukraine. Expansion of sanctions on Russian media disseminating misinformation is approved.
MEPs remain worried about persistently h: MEPs called for the European Central Bank to develop a geopolitical plan assessing the impact of conflicts on price stability, and expressed concerns about high inflation affecting lower-income groups. A decision on introducing a digital euro was not specified.
The European Parliament adopted by 472 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (472-75, 92 abstentions) to strengthen Horizon Europe, the research framework program, emphasizing its importance for Europe's competitiveness. While praising its crisis responsiveness, they expressed concern over insufficient funding and recurring budget cuts that could undermine its effectiveness.
The European Parliament adopted by 469 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (469 votes in favor, 162 against) emphasizing the need for an integrated industrial policy to boost EU innovation capacity and address challenges like green and digital transitions.
The European Parliament adopted by 351 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution for the 2025 European Semester, focusing on employment and social priorities. The EU was urged to intensify efforts towards 2030 social goals by implementing the EPSR and promoting upward social convergence. The Commission was asked to analyze the social dimension in national fiscal plans for the 2026 Joint Employment Report.
Provide more arms and ammunitions to Ukr: Parliament approved measures to increase military aid for Ukraine, with preparations resembling wartime conditions. MEPs endorsed the ReArm plan and called for creative financing options.
The European Parliament adopted by 377 v: The European Parliament approved, by a majority of 377 votes to 279, a regulation strengthening the security of identity cards and residence documents for EU citizens. The approval is subject to amendments requiring compliance with ICAO document 9303 specifications regarding data elements on identity cards.
2026 budget should bolster EU defence an: The European Parliament voted to strengthen EU defense and security capabilities in the 2026 budget, focusing on political and strategic autonomy, as well as social, economic, and territorial cohesion. The budget aims to provide stability for Europeans by serving as a significant investment instrument alongside national budgets.
Rise of authoritarianism, totalitarianis: MEPs condemn threats to independent media, civil society, and democracy from rising authoritarianism and populism. They emphasize the need for proper regulation of artificial intelligence to protect human rights. Parliament reaffirms its support for the International Criminal Court and international justice.
The European Parliament adopted by 399 v: The European Parliament approved a report on the Common Security and Defence Policy Annual Report 2024 (399-198), expressing alarm over escalating security threats since WWII, including geopolitical tensions, authoritarian ambitions, terrorism, and malign actors using force. Members expressed concern about the U.S.'s perceived shift on the Russian war of aggression against Ukraine.
The European Parliament adopted, by 378: The European Parliament approved, with 378 votes in favor and 188 against, the report on the implementation of the Common Foreign and Security Policy - Annual Report 2024. The report emphasizes the escalating geopolitical tensions, with autocratic powers challenging the rules-based international order.
Postponement is part of EU simplificatio: The European Parliament postpones the implementation of new due diligence rules for large companies by one year, with some companies granted an additional two years for sustainability reporting.
The European Parliament adopted by 598 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to allocate €7,999,015 from the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund to support workers affected by redundancies at Van Hool NV in Belgium, providing 85% of the total cost.
The European Parliament adopted by 473 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution aimed at protecting the EU's financial interests and combating fraud, with 473 votes in favor, 59 against, and 114 abstentions. The report revealed a 9% increase in fraud cases from 2022 to 2023, reaching an all-time high.
The European Parliament adopted by 354 v: The European Parliament approved a regulation to suspend parts of EU import rules for Ukrainian products, aiming to support Ukraine amidst Russia's aggression. The vote passed with 354 votes in favor, 147 against, and 53 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 392 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to maintain and potentially strengthen the cohesion policy model, focusing on regional and local levels, as the EU's primary long-term investment instrument for reducing disparities and promoting sustainable growth. Members also advocated for a clear distinction between cohesion policy and other EU instruments.
The European Parliament adopted by 358 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on competition policy for 2024, emphasizing its role in supporting sustainability, energy, defense, and digitalization. The Parliament endorsed the Commission's plan to introduce a new State aid framework for the Clean Industrial Deal, ensuring it fosters innovation without raising prices or reducing market competition.
MEPs want to reduce national filling tar: MEPs voted to lower national gas storage targets, aiming to alleviate market tensions. The proposal includes an 83% storage filling obligation with flexibility to maintain energy security, while advocating for a complete embargo on Russian gas.
Identifying and addressing security risk: The European Parliament voted to make foreign investment screening mandatory across all Member States, granting the European Commission the power to independently address potential security risks associated with both direct and indirect investments.
The European Parliament adopted by 306 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, 306-80, emphasizing the need for ambitious reforms in the single market to address ongoing and emerging challenges without compromising competitiveness. The resolution underscores the importance of the single market for European economic integration and encourages the Commission and Member States to expedite reform efforts and eliminate unjustified barriers to free trade.
The European Parliament adopted by 374 v: The European Parliament approved, by 374 votes to 176, an update to Council Decision 2003/17/EC, allowing field inspections and equivalence of fodder and seed production in Moldova and Ukraine for certain crops.
The European Parliament adopted by 260 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on the 2023 Committee on Petitions deliberations, with a majority vote. The number of petitions increased by 16.2% in 2023 compared to 2022, with most submitted via the Petitions Web Portal.
The European Parliament adopted by 622 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution to strengthen EU rural areas through cohesion policy, addressing their significant challenges. Despite occupying 83% of the EU territory and housing a quarter of its population, rural areas receive less funding compared to urban areas under cohesion policy.
The European Parliament decided, by 653: The European Parliament unanimously passed an amendment requiring rapporteurs to disclose interest representatives and public officials from third countries who influenced their reports or opinions. Article 7 rules apply accordingly.
Montenegro leading candidate country but: Montenegro's EU candidacy status confirmed, with emphasis on advancements in electoral, judicial reforms, and combating organized crime and corruption. Moldova progresses in EU accession, overcoming internal and external challenges.
Persistent, systemic, and new threats to: The European Parliament voted to address persistent threats to the rule of law, addressing concerns over non-compliance with court rulings, civic space erosion, and attacks on EU values. They called for enhanced enforcement tools and immediate action.
Recovery and Resilience Facility’s expir: MEPs voted to extend the Recovery and Resilience Facility by 18 months to complete mature projects. They also emphasized the need for improved transparency and focus on infrastructure, energy, and EU defense investments.
Need for regulatory simplification and a: The European Parliament voted to support regulatory simplification and faster permitting procedures, aiming to establish lead markets for clean technology in Europe. The goal is to protect the European market from unfair competition.
The European Parliament adopted by 511 v: The European Parliament, in a vote of 511 to 14, with 25 abstentions, passed a resolution condemning the dissolution of political parties and repression of the opposition in Mali. The resolution, tabled by multiple groups, expressed concern over Mali's deteriorating political and security situation, and urged the Malian authorities to uphold international human rights law.
The European Parliament adopted by 417 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (417-188) on the European Investment Bank's 2024 annual report, emphasizing support for the bank's role as a climate bank, digitalization, defense industry, modern cohesion policy, agriculture, social infrastructure, global investments, and Capital Markets Union. The resolution also urged the EIB to address a €800 billion investment gap in Europe.
The European Parliament adopted by 531 v: The European Parliament approved Bulgaria's adoption of the euro from January 1, 2026, with 531 votes in favor and 69 against, following a consultation procedure. Once confirmed by Member States, Bulgaria will join the Eurozone as its 21st member.
90% storage filling obligation extended: The European Parliament voted to extend the gas storage filling obligation by two years, ending in 2027, with annual targets to be met between October 1 and December 1. The decision includes flexibility for difficult market conditions and increased transparency on Russian gas sources.
The European Parliament adopted by 422 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to advance Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) implementation by 2025, aiming to meet the targets set in the 2030 Agenda. Despite progress on 17% of SDG targets, many remain off-track.
12 million small e-commerce packages arr: The European Parliament voted to encourage the use of AI and blockchain technologies for customs purposes, and proposed a €2 handling fee for individual e-commerce packages, subject to WTO compliance and not borne by EU consumers. The aim is to incentivize bulk imports and convince non-EU platforms to establish warehouses within the EU.
Austria, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Mold: In a vote, the European Parliament approved aid for Austria, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Moldova, and Bosnia and Herzegovina following 2024 floods. The aid will support infrastructure repair, temporary housing, cultural heritage protection, and cleanup efforts, while emphasizing climate change adaptation and mitigation measures.
Rigged October 2024 elections a clear tu: The European Parliament has rejected the legitimacy of Georgia's one-party parliament and presidency, citing concerns over the October 2024 elections that could lead to an authoritarian government, potentially jeopardizing Georgia's EU accession path.
The European Parliament adopted by 507 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (507-77) condemning Russia's unprovoked war against Ukraine, demanding an immediate ceasefire, withdrawal from occupied territories, and the release of illegally detained civilians and prisoners of war. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups, including EPP, S&D, ECR, Renew, and Greens/EFA.
The European Parliament adopted by 542 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by a large majority, to amend battery due diligence policies. The change delays the implementation of these obligations by two years (until 2027), providing economic operators more time to prepare and address challenges.
The European Parliament adopted by 432 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (432-95) to modernize and strengthen the EU public procurement framework, focusing on boosting competitiveness, promoting sustainability, digitalizing procedures, simplifying rules, ensuring supply security, creating quality jobs, and respecting labor laws.
The European Parliament adopted by 519 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, with 519 votes in favor and 99 against, supporting the use of Cohesion Policy for a just transition towards a carbon-neutral economy by 2050. The resolution emphasizes creating special economic zones under the Just Transition Fund (JTF) to mitigate job losses in affected regions and prioritize funding for vulnerable populations.
The European Parliament adopted by 459 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (459-65) to enhance EU internal market rules, aiming to strengthen the single market and address gaps in global competitiveness. The focus is on removing obstacles for growth, job creation, and investment, while promoting innovation and maintaining high social and environmental standards.
The European Parliament adopted by 388 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on the 2024 European elections, with a record high turnout of 50.74%. Despite variations among Member States, there's a need for increased efforts to boost voter participation across the Union.
The European Parliament adopted by 662 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 662 votes in favor, 3 against, and 2 abstentions, to amend Regulation (EU) 2015/848 on insolvency proceedings, updating Annexes A and B to align with member states' insolvency laws. Slovakia's recent changes to its national law regarding a new preventive restructuring procedure were acknowledged.
Revise current rules on travellers’ refu: Parliament voted to revise traveler refund rights and disruption assistance rules, including tour operator bankruptcy cases. The decision aims to standardize refund vouchers, improve trip cancellation rights, and clarify what comprises a travel package.
Larger, standalone budget for the common: The European Parliament approved a self-contained budget expansion for the Common Agricultural Policy (CAP), focusing on strengthened direct payments and lessening administrative burdens for farmers, while also easing entry barriers into farming.
Funding can be channelled to new priorit: The European Parliament voted to prioritize funding for defense industrial capacity, military mobility, affordable housing, water resilience, dual-use investments, decarbonisation, strategic technologies, and energy interconnectors. Funding rule of law conditionality remains unchanged.
The European Parliament adopted by 407 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 407 votes in favor, 161 against, and 97 abstentions, to support investment and reforms aimed at boosting European competitiveness and creating a Capital Markets Union (Draghi Report). The report emphasized a need for additional annual investment of €750-€800 billion to enhance productivity and meet environmental and social goals. The current investment landscape in the Union is fragmented, with disparities in private finance access, particularly for innovative and high-growth companies. Members urged action to strengthen EU investment through legal, administrative, and financial resources.
The European Parliament adopted by 412 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on employment policies for Member States in 2025, maintaining guidelines outlined in Decision (EU) 2024/3134. The decision also incorporates elements related to geopolitical changes, the Union of Skills initiative, and other updates.
The European Parliament adopted by 578 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to mobilize €931,690 from the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund for 417 Belgian workers made redundant at BelGaN BV, with 578 votes in favor, 47 against, and 16 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 552 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 552 votes in favor and 52 against, to reinforce its commitment to a sustainable and inclusive European Sport Model. The resolution emphasizes efforts to promote safe, equitable sport for all, with a focus on gender equality, combating violence, and improving accessibility.
Compulsory written contracts for deliver: Voters approved mandatory written contracts for agricultural product delivery, with certain exemptions. Stricter labeling and advertising rules for these goods were also passed. Preference was given to locally and seasonally sourced EU products in public procurement.
The European Parliament adopted by 414 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, 414-139, enhancing transnational employee rights in European Works Councils, aiming for effective information and consultation at appropriate management levels.
More flexibility for farmers for keeping: Voters approved measures to increase flexibility for farmers to maintain land in an agricultural and environmentally sound state, and introduced mandatory crisis payments for farmers impacted by natural disasters, with increased support for small farmers.
The European Parliament adopted by 360 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on the Second World Summit for Social Development, aiming to address persistent issues like income inequality, gender disparities, housing insecurity, and regional imbalances that threaten social cohesion within the EU. The EU is committed to implementing the UN's 2030 Sustainable Development Goals through transformative policies.
Parliament condemns violations of EU air: The European Parliament voted to condemn violations of EU airspace and infrastructure interference, advocating for coordinated and proportionate action, including potential downing of airborne threats. They also called for a unified EU-NATO defense, sanctions on Russia, advancement of the European Defence Union, and cooperation with Ukraine.
The European Parliament adopted by 471 v: The European Parliament passed a regulation to establish a common data platform on chemicals, aiming for efficient and consistent hazard and risk assessments to protect human health and the environment.
The European Parliament adopted by 477 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 477 votes in favor, 93 against, and 76 abstentions, to amend Directive 2011/65/EU on hazardous substances in electronic equipment, expanding the responsibilities of the European Chemicals Agency. The amendment modifies Articles 5 and 6 of the directive.
The European Parliament adopted by 469 v: The European Parliament passed a regulation, with 469 votes in favor, 126 against, and 50 abstentions, to improve cooperation among chemical agencies and reassign certain tasks within the European Food Safety Authority (EFSA), aiming to align scientific opinions.
The European Parliament adopted by 505 v: The European Parliament approved a revised product legislative framework for digital and sustainable transition, passing with 505 votes in favor, 62 against, and 47 abstentions. The update aims to adapt the EU's regulatory framework to technological advancements, digitalization, sustainability, and market changes.
The European Parliament adopted by 446 v: The European Parliament passed a regulation on the statute and funding of European political parties, with 446 votes in favor, 162 against, and 35 abstentions. The adopted text requires political alliances to uphold EU values and prohibits membership for parties or individuals subject to restrictive measures.
The European Parliament adopted by 310 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 310 votes in favor, 277 against, and 53 abstentions, advocating for continued EU enlargement, emphasizing its long-term geostrategic importance amidst Russia's aggression against Ukraine and other geopolitical challenges. The resolution highlights the need to continue the accession process with the nine candidate countries, despite Georgia's unilateral suspension of negotiations in November 2024.
The European Parliament adopted by 367 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (367-98, 113 abstentions) to renew the EU-Africa partnership, emphasizing mutual respect, shared interests, and the need for cooperation in a changing geopolitical landscape. The partnership aims to address growing global insecurity and extremism.
The European Parliament adopted by 382 v: The European Parliament passed an amendment to corporate sustainability reporting and due diligence requirements, affecting companies with over 1000 employees and €450 million in annual sales. The amendment was referred back for further negotiations.
The EU must respond to the growing numbe: The European Parliament voted to impose sanctions and ban exports of spyware and dual-use goods to countries involved in transnational repression, targeting the 10 regimes responsible for 80% of such attacks on EU soil.
Gender-based violence should be an EU cr: The European Parliament voted to make gender-based violence a EU criminal offense, affirming the need for full implementation of EU equality legislation. They also supported recognizing safe and legal abortion as a fundamental right, and advocated for a consent-based definition of rape in EU law.
The European Parliament adopted by 379 v: The European Parliament passed an amendment to strengthen climate neutrality goals, aiming for a 90% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions by 2040 compared to 1990 levels. Further discussions on post-2030 targets are ongoing.
Grave concern over physical and mental h: MEPs voted to strengthen EU digital rules, focusing on protecting minors online. Measures include fines and potential bans for non-compliant platforms, bans on engagement-based recommender algorithms and loot boxes in games, and action on generative AI tools like deepfakes and nudity apps.
The European Parliament adopted by 519 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (519-65) to enhance the EU guarantee under Regulation (EU) 2021/523 and simplify reporting requirements. The move aims to optimize resources in the InvestEU program, addressing market failures and accelerating the EU's green and digital transition.
The European Parliament adopted by 330 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, 330-273, to support the Draghi report outlining Europe's future growth strategy. The strategy focuses on innovation, decarbonisation, security, and environmental/social objectives, requiring an estimated €750-€800 billion in annual investments.
The European Parliament adopted by 360 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (360-298) to improve access to finance for SMEs and scale-ups, aiming to create favorable market conditions for European businesses to innovate and grow. The resolution addresses regulatory fragmentation as a barrier to exploiting the single market's potential, and calls for tailored solutions to address unequal financing conditions across Member States and regions.
Ambitious and effective updated strategy: The European Parliament voted to approve an updated strategy for disability rights from 2025 to 2030, emphasizing equal rights for all EU citizens. A unified definition of "disability" was proposed to address disparities, particularly focusing on women and girls with disabilities.
EU-wide transparency rules detailing int: The European Parliament passed new transparency rules for interest representation services, funding, and third-country links, aiming to reduce fragmentation, boost public trust, and strengthen protection against foreign interference. These rules also seek to safeguard registered entities from stigmatization.
The European Parliament adopted by 521 v: The European Parliament approved, with 521 votes in favor, 110 against, and 35 abstentions, a resolution to amend data collection regulations for the European Central Bank. The approval includes amendments suggesting the ECB should further explore digital tools to simplify data collection processes.
The European Parliament adopted by 337 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, with 337 votes in favor, 245 against, and 12 abstentions, emphasizing the importance of subsidiarity and proportionality principles in EU legislation. The Parliament stressed that these principles, ensuring decisions are made closest to citizens, should be integral to the Commission's regulatory improvement approach, which includes evaluation, impact assessment, and stakeholder consultation. These principles are crucial for maintaining the division of powers and ensuring EU action is necessary and appropriate.
Boost to European defence capabilities\n: The European Parliament voted to strengthen European defense capacities, promoting a "Buy European" strategy. This decision includes increased aid for Ukraine.
The European Parliament adopted by 452 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 452 votes to 182, against renewing the authorization for genetically modified cotton MON 88913 in products, excluding food and feed, as per Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003. The application for renewal, submitted by Bayer Agriculture B.V., was rejected.
