The European Parliament adopted by 522 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to update the EU's 2024 budget, including revenue adjustments and expenditure changes for certain agencies, and a reduced UK contribution of €2.38 billion.
The new loan is the EU’s contribution to: The European Parliament approved a loan for Ukraine, with repayment sourced from windfall profits of frozen Russian assets, until 2025.
The European Parliament decided to refus: The European Parliament voted to withhold discharge to the Secretary-General of the Council for the 2022 budget implementation, due to the Council's persistent lack of cooperation on the discharge procedure since 2009. The vote passed with 431 in favor, 131 against, and 83 abstentions.
Support the status quo in the Taiwan Str: The European Parliament voted to maintain the current status quo in the Taiwan Strait, rejecting any actions that contradict international law, while acknowledging that UN resolution 2758 does not address Taiwan's political status.
Parliament condemns repression of activi: The European Parliament condemns repression of activists, journalists, and opposition leaders globally, including in Azerbaijan. The Parliament finds Azerbaijan's human rights abuses incompatible with its hosting of COP29, and calls for the withdrawal of troops from Armenian territory.
The European Parliament adopted by 555 v: The European Parliament passed, with 555 votes in favor and 48 against, a resolution to adopt a directive for Faster and Safer Relief of Excess Withholding Taxes. The directive introduces a common EU digital tax residence certificate (eTRC) for tax-paying investors, allowing them to benefit from expedited procedures to avoid withholding taxes. Member States will issue eTRCs through an automated process.
The European Parliament adopted by 510 v: The European Parliament, through a special legislative procedure, approved a resolution to amend the European Investment Bank's Statute, aiming to remove statutory lending limits from the bank's statutes, following a G20-commissioned review. The vote passed with 510 in favor, 105 against, and 27 abstentions.
Second von der Leyen Commission approved: The European Parliament approved the second Von der Leyen Commission, with 370 votes in favor, 282 against, and 36 abstentions. The Commission is expected to take office on 1 December, marking the start of five years of monitoring and enforcing their commitments.
Next year’s EU budget signed into law by: The European Parliament has approved a €199.4 billion budget for 2025, increasing spending by 6% over the current year, with additional funds of over €230 million allocated for key programs aimed at enhancing people's lives, boosting competitiveness, and addressing current challenges.
The European Parliament adopted by 474 v: The European Parliament rejected the renewal of authorization for genetically modified maize products, with 474 votes in favor, 171 against, and 13 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 476 v: The European Parliament rejected the renewal of authorization for genetically modified maize products, with 476 votes in favor, 169 against, and 14 abstentions. The products in question are MON 89034 × 1507 × MON 88017 × 59122 and eight of its sub-combinations. The decision was made pursuant to Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003.
The European Parliament adopted by 478 v: The European Parliament rejected the authorization for genetically modified maize DP202216 in food, feed, and ingredients, passing with 478 votes against 165, and 16 abstentions. The application was submitted by Pioneer Hi-Bred International, Inc., based in the US.
The European Parliament adopted by 525 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to adjust the EU budget for 2024, increasing payment appropriations by €2.95 billion and commitment appropriations by €44.5 million.
The European Parliament adopted by 474 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 474 votes in favor and 166 against, objecting to the use of genetically modified maize DP23211 in food, ingredients, and feed, following an application by Pioneer Hi-Bred International, Inc. (US).
The European Parliament adopted by 478 v: The European Parliament rejected the authorization for genetically modified maize DP915635 in food, feed, and ingredients, passing with 478 votes against 166, and 17 abstentions. The decision concerns an application by Pioneer Hi-Bred International, based in the US.
Support from the EU Solidarity Fund afte: EU Parliament approved aid for flood-stricken regions in Bavaria, Baden-Württemberg, and Valle d'Aosta. The decision includes financial assistance for emergency and recovery operations. MEPs advocated for increased EU aid due to the rising frequency of natural disasters in Europe.
The European Parliament adopted by 627 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 627 votes in favor and 37 abstentions, to amend Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 regarding Vanuatu's citizenship investment schemes due to security concerns. The amendment was adopted at first reading, aligning with the Commission's proposal.
The Georgian parliamentary elections mus: The European Parliament calls for a re-run of Georgian parliamentary elections under international supervision. It also advocates for EU sanctions against Georgia's Prime Minister and top officials due to policy incompatibilities with Euro-Atlantic integration.
The European Parliament adopted by 562 v: The European Parliament, by a large majority, passed a resolution condemning the increasing repression of women in Iran, including the detention and psychiatric confinement of Ahoo Daryaei. The resolution calls for the repeal of discriminatory laws against women and girls in Iran.
The European Parliament adopted by 473 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 473 votes in favor, condemning the deterioration of fundamental freedoms and the rule of law in Hong Kong, particularly the cases of Jimmy Lai and 45 activists convicted under the National Security Law. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups, including EPP, S&D, ECR, Renew, and Greens/EFA.
The European Parliament adopted by 492 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 492 votes in favor, condemning the shrinking civic space in Cambodia, particularly the case of labor rights organization CENTRAL. The resolution calls for the immediate release of political prisoners, activists, journalists, and human rights defenders, and demands an end to repression and harassment against civil society actors. Cambodian authorities are urged to respect these demands.
Pyongyang’s involvement and Russia’s tes: The European Parliament calls for an end to military and dual-use assistance from China to Russia, and advocates for additional sanctions against strategically important sectors in Russia, in response to Russia's testing of ballistic missiles and Pyongyang's involvement escalating the war situation. This aligns with the EU-US shared strategic interest in supporting Ukraine.
Regional development funds can be used m: The European Parliament voted to expand the use of regional development funds for disaster reconstruction, including healthcare and basic necessities. Compensation for losses in agriculture and forestry sectors was also approved.
The European Parliament adopted by 598 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, passing with a large majority, commending the European Ombudsman's 2023 annual report and acknowledging Emily O'Reilly's significant contributions to EU democracy through increased accountability and transparency. The Parliament also praised the cooperative relationship with the Ombudsman's office.
The European Parliament adopted by 499 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, 499-131, to amend forest reproductive material regulations, extending the equivalence regime for the 'tested' category, aligning with 2013 OECD rules.
The European Parliament adopted by 591 v: The European Parliament approved, with a majority of 591 votes, the legislative resolution on the UN Convention on Transparency in Investor-State Arbitration. The agreement was consented to, applying to investment treaties before April 2014. The convention establishes a transparency mechanism for disputes covered by these treaties.
The European Parliament adopted by 533 v: The European Parliament approved the accession of Tuvalu to the Interim Partnership Agreement with the Pacific States, providing continued duty-free-quota-free access to the EU market, as it prepares to graduate from Least Developed Country status in 2024.
The European Parliament adopted by 543 v: The European Parliament approved, with 543 votes in favor and 77 against, the accession of Niue to the Interim Partnership Agreement with the EU and Pacific States. This move allows Niue to benefit from a significant portion of duty elimination or reductions in EU tariffs. To access full duty-free-quota-free market access and additional Agreement benefits, Niue must accede to the interim EU-Pacific States Economic Partnership Agreement.
The European Parliament adopted by 448 v: The European Parliament approved the establishment of two standing committees: Security and Defence, and Public Health. The Security and Defence committee will oversee EU security, defence integration, and cooperation, including the European Defence Agency and CSDP.
The European Parliament adopted by 492 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 492 votes in favor, expressing concern over the arrest and detention of Temirlan Sultanbekov, leader of the Kyrgyzstan Social Democrats party, and other officials, amid allegations of vote-buying. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups, including EPP, S&D, ECR, Renew, Greens/EFA, and The Left, and called for an investigation into the case, as Sultanbekov's health has deteriorated while on hunger strike.
The European Parliament adopted by 446 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 446 votes in favor, 25 against, and 51 abstentions, condemning the 11-year Russian occupation of Crimea and Sevastopol, and highlighting deteriorating human rights, including cases of Iryna Danylovych, Tofik Abdulhaziiev, and Amet Suleymanov. The resolution was tabled by multiple parliament groups.
The European Parliament adopted by 505 v: The European Parliament approved, by a large majority, an agreement authorizing the European Border and Coast Guard Agency to operate in Serbia, following the recommendation of the Civil Liberties, Justice and Home Affairs committee. The Council decision is intended to formalize the Status Agreement on EU-Serbia border actions.
The EU should refuse to recognise Aliaks: The European Parliament voted to not recognize Alexander Lukashenko's anticipated victory in Belarus, and supports broadening EU sanctions against Belarusian authorities in support of the country's pursuit of democracy and human rights.
Expand sanctions to Nicolás Maduro and h: The European Parliament voted to extend sanctions against Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro and his associates. The decision was made due to the lack of democratic legitimacy in Venezuela, and the unjustified arrest warrant for Edmundo Gonzalez Urrutia was called for revocation.
The European Parliament adopted by 533 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 533 votes in favor, 24 against, and 48 abstentions, condemning the arrest and detention of French-Algerian writer Boualem Sansal, who was arrested for criticizing Algeria's authoritarian regime. The resolution calls for Sansal's immediate release and respect for his right to freedom of expression.
The European Parliament adopted by 556 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 556 votes in favor and 42 abstentions, condemning human rights violations in Iran, including the death sentences of Pakhshan Azizi and Wrisha Moradi. The resolution also addressed the detention of EU citizens as hostages. The resolution was supported by multiple groups, including EPP, S&D, ECR, Renew, and Greens/EFA.
The European Parliament adopted by 562 v: The European Parliament approved, with a large majority, an agreement on air services between the EU and Bangladesh. The agreement, signed in June 2024, replaces parts of existing bilateral air service agreements.
Fishers from Spain, France and Portugal: The European Parliament approved a deal to support sustainable fishing in Cabo Verde, providing €780,000 annually to fishermen from Spain, France, and Portugal, while also implementing measures against illegal fishing.
The European Parliament adopted by 591 v: The European Parliament approved, with minor dissent, the renewal of the Agreement on science and technology cooperation between the EU and Ukraine. The Parliament consented to the extension of the agreement.
The European Parliament adopted by 589 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, 589-42, to update Value Added Tax (VAT) rules for the digital age, adopting the Council draft without amendment. The proposal introduces new rules for electronic invoices, real-time data reporting, and commercial activities on digital platforms.
The European Parliament adopted by 608 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 608 votes in favor and 33 against, to amend Directive 2011/16/EU on taxation cooperation. The proposal, linked to the 2022 Pillar 2 Directive, simplifies reporting obligations for multinational enterprises and establishes a system for cross-authority information exchange.
The European Parliament adopted by 467 v: The European Parliament rejected, by a majority of 467 votes to 156, a resolution allowing the sale of genetically modified maize MON 95275, following an application by Bayer Agriculture B.V. The decision was made under Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003.
Occupation of Goma breaches the Democrat: The European Parliament voted to condemn the occupation of Goma, supporting immediate resumption of humanitarian operations and advocating for prosecution of war crime perpetrators, while endorsing the Luanda and Nairobi peace processes.
Parliament recognises Salome Zourabichvi: Parliament confirmed Salome Zourabichvili as Georgia's legitimate president. A call for EU sanctions against key Georgian politicians was proposed due to the ongoing political crisis, with new elections suggested as a solution.
New €1.9 billion facility to accelerate: The European Parliament approved a €1.9 billion fund to boost Moldova's socio-economic reforms, enhance resilience, and further EU integration, aimed at mitigating the effects of Russia's aggression against Ukraine. The agreement provides more grants, faster funding, and increased parliamentary oversight.
The European Parliament adopted by 643 v: The European Parliament approved, with a large majority, a decision to verify the credentials of its members, resolving any disputes related to the 1976 Act on the election of MEPs by direct universal suffrage. All member states have reported the names of elected members.
The European Parliament adopted by 521 v: The European Parliament approved, by a large majority, an agreement to transfer Passenger Name Record data to Canada for crime and terrorism prevention, ensuring respect for EU fundamental rights.
The European Parliament adopted by 525 v: The European Parliament approved an agreement amending the EU-Norway trade deal due to Brexit, with 525 votes in favor, 5 against, and 34 abstentions. The agreement was consented to by the Parliament.
Sufficient resources for education, trai: MEPs voted to maintain the independence of the ESF+, rejecting changes to its governance and structure to preserve its focus on education, training, and skills development.
MEPs condemn the retaliatory targeting o: MEPs condemn the targeting of Alawite community members and call for a pluralistic political transition, including all religious, ethnic groups, women, civil society, and peaceful opposition forces. They also urge increased humanitarian aid and the use of Assad's frozen assets for reconstruction. (Passed)
MEPs welcome proposal for a 30-day cease: MEPs endorse a 30-day ceasefire proposal for Ukraine, urging Russia to comply. They also support increased aid for Ukraine and a potential European-led coalition for enforcing a future peace agreement.
The European Parliament adopted by 482 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 482 votes in favor and 57 against, condemning Thailand's lese-majesty law and the deportation of Uyghur refugees. The resolution, tabled by multiple groups, also criticized the Thai authorities for violating international law by deporting at least 40 Uyghur refugees to China. Additionally, it expressed concern over the persecution of over 1960 pro-democracy activists, human rights defenders, and journalists since 2020.
The European Parliament adopted by 575 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 575 votes to 7, addressing the ongoing crisis in Sudan, focusing on sexual violence and child rape. The resolution aims to provide aid to the 13 million displaced people and the majority of Sudan's population in need of humanitarian assistance.
The European Parliament adopted by 523 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 523 votes in favor and 84 abstentions, condemning the unlawful detention and sham trials of Armenian hostages, including high-ranking political representatives from Nagorno-Karabakh, by Azerbaijan. The resolution, tabled by multiple groups, demands the release of these Armenian hostages, who are facing illegal life imprisonment sentences.
The European Parliament adopted by 619 v: The European Parliament approved, by a large majority, an agreement to strengthen judicial cooperation between Eurojust and Bosnia and Herzegovina's authorities, aiming to more effectively combat serious crime. The agreement was consented to by the Parliament.
Vessels from Spain, Portugal, Italy, Gre: The European Parliament approved a fishing agreement, allowing vessels from Spain, Portugal, Italy, Greece, and France to fish in Guinea-Bissau's waters for tuna, cephalopods, and crustaceans until 2029. The agreement includes provisions for EU aid to combat illegal fishing and "flag-of-convenience" practices.
The European Parliament adopted by 528 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, passing by a majority of 528 votes, to adopt financial mechanism agreements with Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and the EU for the period 2021-2028.
The European Parliament adopted by 593 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 593 votes in favor and 7 against, to amend customs duties on certain US imports. The amendment introduces a de minimis threshold for retaliation against the US, following a WTO case loss in 2003.
The European Parliament adopted by 432 v: The European Parliament approved a budget of €2,636,241,620 for 2026, marking a 4.09% increase over the 2025 budget, following an agreement reached on March 18, 2025.
Urgent need to address the persecution o:
MEPs call for action to protect journalists in Cameroon and secure press freedom before the presidential election.

European Parliament urges an end to Iran's execution of political prisoners, including EU nationals, and the freezing of Lukashenka's assets to aid victims of repression in Belarus.
The European Parliament adopted by 340 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (340 votes in favor, 162 against, 150 abstentions) on the EIB's 2023 annual report, emphasizing the bank's effective performance amid global challenges. The EIB, a key player in the EU's financial architecture, was urged to address competitiveness issues like energy price volatility, skills gaps, and insufficient investment in innovation and technology.
Post-2027 budget must reflect current ge: The European Parliament voted to reject a rigid post-2027 budget based on a single national plan, favoring a flexible budget that reflects current geopolitical, economic, and environmental realities. A call for robust parliamentary accountability to manage and track funds was also emphasized.
Normalisation of Belgrade-Pristina relat: The European Parliament voted to emphasize the importance of normalizing Belgrade-Pristina relations. A call was made for an official investigation into the Novi Sad train station canopy collapse. Kosovo was urged to intensify efforts against foreign interference and disinformation.
Ongoing deterioration of democratic stan: MEPs condemned the harsh crackdown on peaceful mass protests in Turkey, while advocating for deeper EU-Turkey cooperation in strategic areas. Criticism was expressed towards President Erdoğan's illegal visit to the occupied areas of the Republic of Cyprus.
The European Parliament decided to grant: The European Parliament approved discharge for the implementation of the budget for 31 out of 33 decentralized agencies and two other bodies (EIT and ESA) for the financial year 2023. The discharge for the EU Asylum Agency was postponed.
Introduce water efficiency targets and s: Vote Outcome: Water efficiency targets and climate adaptation measures were approved, aiming to enhance resilience. Funding for essential water projects and digitalization were also supported for innovation.
The European Parliament adopted by 419 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on Serbia's EU membership aspirations, emphasizing the need for swift reform implementation to align with EU acquis by 2026. The Parliament reiterated the strategic importance of the Western Balkans for EU security and stability, while condemning actions contrary to EU values.
The European Parliament adopted by 345 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on the Banking Union for 2024, aiming to secure the stability of the banking sector and prevent taxpayer bailouts. The resolution emphasizes the importance of a robust banking sector for economic growth, home ownership, investment, job creation, SME financing, and the transition to a green and digital economy.
The European Parliament adopted by 373 v: The European Parliament rejected a decision to allow genetically modified soybean MON 87705 × MON 87708 × MON 89788 in markets, with 373 votes against, 150 for, and 10 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 516 v: The European Parliament, by a significant majority, passed a resolution condemning the forced transfer and deportation of Ukrainian children by Russia. Over 20,000 children have been affected since February 2022, with over 600 confirmed deaths and thousands more unaccounted for.
The European Parliament adopted by 478 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by a majority, addressing religious freedom violations in Tibet, expressing concern over the death of Tulku Hungkar Dorje under suspicious circumstances. The resolution emphasizes the need for respect for human rights, democracy, and the rule of law in EU-China relations.
The European Parliament adopted by 489 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to extend the Euratom Research and Training Programme for 2026-2027, complementing Horizon Europe, for the remaining two years of the current multiannual financial framework (MFF). The proposal was adopted with 489 votes in favor, 55 against, and 38 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 411 v: The European Parliament approved a regulation to increase customs duties on agricultural imports from Russia and Belarus by 50%, aiming to reduce dependency and prevent circumvention via Belarus. The proposal passed with 411 votes in favor, 100 against, and 78 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 564 v: The European Parliament passed an amendment to strengthen the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), aimed at reducing compliance burden and improving functionality. A new 50-tonne threshold was introduced.
Higher maximum penalties for several for: Parliament approved increased penalties for various forms of abuse and abolished statutes of limitations to allow victims more time to report. A revised consent definition for minors above the age of consent was also adopted.
The European Parliament adopted, by 587: The European Parliament approved, by a large majority, a measure to simplify voting rights for EU citizens residing in a non-native country. The proposal, based on existing laws, was agreed upon by all parties after consultations.
The European Parliament adopted by 554 v: The European Parliament approved, with 554 votes in favor, 53 against, and 37 abstentions, a resolution adopting the Energy Charter Treaty interpretation agreement. The Parliament endorsed the Commission's proposal, acknowledging the Court of Justice of the European Union's ruling that Article 26(2)(c) of the Energy Charter Treaty does not apply to disputes between a Member State and an investor.
The European Parliament adopted, by 583: The European Parliament approved, by a large margin, the termination of the Voluntary Partnership Agreement on forest law enforcement, governance, and timber trade with Cameroon. The agreement, signed in 2010, was consented to be ended.
The European Parliament adopted, by 557: The European Parliament approved, with a large majority, a resolution to terminate the Voluntary Partnership Agreement with Cameroon on forest law enforcement and timber trade, citing non-compliance over the past decade.
EU-Ukraine road transport agreement exte: The EU-Ukraine road transport agreement was extended to end-2025, facilitating the transport of essential goods into Ukraine and export of grain and steel. Suspension is possible in case of significant disruptions to local road transport markets.
€4 billion worth of financial aid in loa: Parliament approved €4 billion in loans for Egypt's reforms, aimed at supporting structural, political, and economic changes. Further reporting on fund usage is required by the Commission.
Mandatory microchipping and registration: The European Parliament voted to pass regulations requiring microchipping and registration of all dogs and cats within the EU, prohibiting the sale of dogs and cats in pet shops, and mandating registration for imported dogs and cats. The new rules aim to regulate the €1.3 billion annual EU dog and cat trade, with many owners purchasing pets online.
The European Parliament adopted by 557 v: The European Parliament approved, by a significant majority, a resolution adopting the Commission's proposed agreement interpreting and applying the Energy Charter Treaty between the EU, EURATOM, and their member states. The agreement clarifies the parties' common understanding on various aspects of the treaty.
Better integration of national renewable: The European Parliament voted to enhance national renewable energy integration for a resilient, decarbonised electricity system. The decision includes increased investment and simplified permit procedures to modernise grid infrastructure, addressing grid resilience issues.
The European Parliament adopted by 324 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 324 votes in favor and 25 against, urging the immediate and unconditional release of journalist Mzia Amaglobeli in Georgia, following her arrest during pro-European protests. The resolution also condemned her politically motivated arrest and prosecution.
The European Parliament adopted by 486 v: The European Parliament, in a vote of 486 to 8 with 29 abstentions, passed a resolution urging Iran to immediately release Dr. Ahmadreza Djalali, a Swedish-Iranian academic, and all political prisoners. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups, calling for Djalali's release, who was arrested in 2016 and sentenced to death on espionage charges.
The European Parliament adopted by 572 v: The European Parliament approved, with 572 votes in favor and 42 against, a resolution to gradually implement the Entry/Exit System (EES), a crucial aspect of Schengen border management, over a 180-day period.
The European Parliament adopted by 623 v: The European Parliament approved a directive to improve VAT collection on imported goods, making suppliers liable for VAT. The directive, adopted with 623 votes in favor, simplifies VAT declaration and payment for cross-border sales.
The European Parliament adopted by 534 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to incorporate a surplus of €1.345 billion from the 2024 financial year into the 2025 budget, with 534 votes in favor, 102 against, and 56 abstentions. The surplus primarily stems from an increase in revenue under Title 4.
The European Parliament adopted by 616 v: The European Parliament approved, with a majority of 616 votes, the consent for the conclusion of the Sustainable Fisheries Partnership Agreement (2025-2030) between the EU, Greenland, and Denmark. The agreement, signed at the end of 2024, allows EU vessels to fish species like cod and Greenland fish.
The European Parliament adopted by 494 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (494-83, 71 abstentions) to strengthen the EU biotechnology and biomanufacturing sector, focusing on research, innovation, and competitiveness enhancement. The Parliament emphasized the sector's growth potential and the importance of maintaining the EU's leading position in this field. Members urged the Commission to address gaps and simplify legislation in proposed EU-wide regulations, particularly reducing administrative burdens.
MEPs praise Albania’s steadfast commitme: The European Parliament urges Albania to combat corruption and uphold the rule of law, praising its commitment to the EU. Reforms and unity are called for in Bosnia and Herzegovina to address corruption and division. North Macedonia is encouraged to persist in its EU efforts amid political challenges and public discontent.
The European Parliament adopted by 574 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to allocate €170,000 from the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund for displaced workers, allowing for technical assistance at the Commission's initiative.
The European Parliament adopted by 681 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 681 votes in favor and 4 against, to amend fishing regulations concerning countries allowing non-sustainable fishing practices, aiming for legal clarity. The amendment to Regulation (EU) No 1026/2012 was adopted at first reading.
The European Parliament adopted by 617 v: The European Parliament, by a large majority, passed a resolution condemning the arbitrary arrest and torture of Belgian-Portuguese researcher Joseph Figueira Martin in the Central African Republic. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups, including EPP, S&D, ECR, Renew, and Greens/EFA.
The European Parliament adopted by 511 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 511 votes in favor and 50 against, urging the UAE to release Ryan Cornelius, a British national detained since 2008 on false charges, due to his deteriorating health and inhumane prison conditions. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups, including EPP, S&D, ECR, Renew, and Greens/EFA.
The European Parliament adopted by 523 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 523 votes in favor and 75 against, aiming to address China's restrictions on critical raw materials exports. The resolution focuses on ensuring a secure and sustainable supply of these essential materials for industries like clean technologies, digital technologies, healthcare, and defense. The EU remains overly dependent on non-EU countries, particularly China, for these critical raw materials.
The European Parliament adopted by 625 v: The European Parliament, in a vote of 625 to 7 with 10 abstentions, passed a resolution condemning the terrorist attack on Mar Elias Church in Damascus and urging protection for religious minorities in Syria. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups following the June 2025 attack that killed at least 25 and injured over 60.
EU is going through a sever housing cris: MEPs have approved measures to address Europe's housing crisis, promoting renovations, conversions, public-private partnerships, and investment in social housing. They aim to increase housing investment through new flexibilities for national and regional authorities.
New threshold will exempt 90% of importe: The European Parliament voted to exempt 90% of importers from the EU carbon border adjustment mechanism (CBAM), while maintaining a 99% coverage for CO2 emissions from iron, steel, aluminum, and cement imports. Simplified procedures for CBAM-covered imports were also approved.
The European Parliament adopted by 461 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, supporting a new vision for European Universities alliances, aiming to enhance cross-border cooperation in higher education. The initiative was welcomed for addressing funding, accreditation, and legal barriers.
MEPs reassert unwavering commitment to U: The European Parliament voted to reinforce support for Ukraine's independence, sovereignty, and territorial integrity. They also called for expedited EU membership negotiations for Ukraine, positioning the EU as a strategic ally.
The European Parliament adopted by 627 v: The European Parliament approved, with a majority of 627 votes, the agreement on Switzerland's cooperation with the EU on the Galileo satellite navigation program. Parliament granted consent to the agreement, strengthening Switzerland's long-term collaboration with the EU on this project.
The European Parliament adopted by 513 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, with 513 votes in favor, 111 against, and 40 abstentions, advocating for simplification of cohesion funds. The Parliament emphasized the significant role of cohesion policy in addressing past five years' emergencies and challenges, but expressed concerns over frequent regulatory changes weakening its strategic focus. Members proposed a permanent, strengthened emergency instrument within the EU budget to respond quickly to crises.
The European Parliament adopted by 634 v: The European Parliament approved, with minimal opposition, the extension of the Eurojust case management system's establishment timeframe until December 2027, allowing the continued use of temporary work files and an index.
T he European Parliament adopted by 612: The European Parliament passed a resolution to shorten the settlement cycle for EU securities transactions from T+2 to T+1, with 612 votes in favor, 7 against, and 45 abstentions. The amended Central Securities Depositories (CSD) Regulation exempts certain transactions from the requirement of a settlement date no later than the first business day after trading.
The European Parliament adopted by 434 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 434 votes in favor and 49 against, expressing concern over human rights, democracy, and rule of law in Kyrgyzstan, while strengthening EU-Kyrgyzstan political and trade relations through the Enhanced Partnership and Cooperation Agreement. The Parliament urged Kyrgyz authorities to respect these concerns.
The European Parliament adopted by 528 v: The European Parliament approved, by a significant majority, the accession of Vanuatu to the Interim Partnership Agreement with Pacific States. This move strengthens the EU's trade framework with Pacific partners and facilitates mutual trade, incorporating Vanuatu into the joint rules and institutions of the agreement.
Binding targets for use of recycled mate: The European Parliament voted to establish mandatory targets for using recycled materials in new vehicles and strengthened producer responsibility for end-of-life vehicle collection and treatment, aiming to improve recycling rates for the EU's annual 6.5 million end-of-life vehicles.
The European Parliament adopted by 490 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 490 votes to 65, supporting Moldova's resistance against Russian hybrid threats and interference. The resolution endorses Moldova's independence, sovereignty, and territorial integrity, and urges Russia to respect these principles.
The European Parliament adopted by 355 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 355 votes to 173, expressing concern over the escalating violence and terrorist attacks in Colombia, linked to groups like ELN and FARC dissidents. The resolution was tabled by EPP, ECR, and Renew groups, addressing the ongoing issues despite the 2016 Peace Agreement.
The European Parliament adopted by 549 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 549 votes in favor and 41 abstentions, urging Rwanda to end harassment and imprisonment of opposition leaders, journalists, and civil society, guarantee press freedom, and ensure democratic participation, following the arrest of Victoire Ingabire.
The European Parliament adopted by 597 t: The European Parliament passed a resolution, unanimously (597-0, 5 abstentions), condemning the detention of EU citizens in occupied Cyprus, specifically five Greek Cypriots in Galatia. The resolution calls for their immediate release and safe return.
MEPs condemn Israel’s humanitarian block: The European Parliament condemned Israel's humanitarian blockade in Gaza, deeming it a cause of famine. The Parliament also stated that Israel's self-defense does not justify indiscriminate military action, and urged member states to consider recognizing the State of Palestine.
New grounds for suspending visa-free acc: The European Parliament voted to expand grounds for suspending visa-free access to the EU, now including hybrid threats, golden passports, human rights violations, and non-compliance with international court decisions.
The European Parliament adopted by 577 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to provide financial aid of €3,085,166 from the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund to workers made redundant at Goodyear Germany GmbH in Kassel and Darmstadt, following a request from Germany. The vote passed with 577 in favor, 47 against, and 15 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted by 636 v: The European Parliament passed a directive, with 636 votes in favor and 10 against, aiming to improve safety, efficiency, and sustainability of inland waterway transport by establishing a framework for harmonized river information services.
The European Parliament adopted by 479 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, passing by a majority of 479 votes, to increase GNI contributions by €4.3 billion in the 2025 EU budget, due to changes in other revenue sources and expenditure needs. The resolution also noted the adjustment of the uniform GNI call rate.
The European Parliament adopted by 354 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to strengthen EU-Latin America strategic partnership, aiming to counter growing influence of China, Russia, and Iran in the region, expressing concern over their proximity to authoritarian regimes.
The European Parliament adopted by 499 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (499-66, 53 abstentions) advocating for a straightforward, competitive, and predictable European tax system to foster business and worker growth during the ecological and digital transition. The resolution also expressed concern over US President's statements regarding VAT in EU Member States, as they could justify tariffs and potentially harm trade relations.
The European Parliament adopted by 594 v: The European Parliament approved, with 594 votes in favor, 38 against, and 2 abstentions, the resolution to withdraw the European Union from the International Rubber Study Group (IRSG). The Parliament consented to the EU's exit from the IRSG, an organization focusing on rubber production, consumption, and trade issues. The EU has been the sole member since 2011.
The European Parliament adopted by 433 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 433 votes in favor and 166 against, objecting to the placement of genetically modified maize DP51291 in the market. The decision was based on an application submitted by Corteva Agriscience Belgium B.V. for foods, feed, and food ingredients containing the maize.
The European Parliament adopted by 504 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 504 votes to 74, to support Afghan women and communities affected by the 2025 earthquake, as winter approaches and aid is hindered by global cuts and Taliban restrictions.
The European Parliament adopted by 550 v: The European Parliament, in a strong show of unity, passed a resolution condemning the rising power of criminal gangs in Haiti, particularly the Cabaret massacre, by 550 votes to 3, with 54 abstentions. The resolution calls for justice system reform to end impunity and international support to address the ongoing gang violence.
The European Parliament adopted by 546 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 546 votes in favor and 44 abstentions, condemning the 10-year imprisonment of Swedish publisher Gui Minhai in China, who was abducted in 2015 for publishing critical works about the Chinese leadership.
The European Parliament adopted by 327 v: The European Parliament approved changes to the Standing Forestry Committee's mandate, aiming to rename it and expand its mission. The proposal now moves to inter-institutional negotiations.
The European Parliament adopted by 533 v: The European Parliament passed, with 533 votes in favor and 43 against, a regulation enhancing procedural rules for handling complaints and investigations in cross-border data protection cases under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). The regulation was amended to establish rules for supervisory authorities.
The European Parliament decided to grant: The European Parliament approved the discharge of the European Union Agency for Asylum's Executive Director for the 2023 budget and closed the accounts for that year. The decision was supported by 476 votes, with 99 votes against and 72 abstentions, following the Court of Auditors' assurance of the reliability and legality of the annual accounts.
Strong condemnation of repression and de: The European Parliament strongly condemned repression and demanded the release of political prisoners in Belarus. They called for new, free and fair elections under international observation, expressing concern over Russia's increasing influence and security threats.
The European Parliament decided, by 441: The European Parliament, by a majority of 441 votes to 125, with 73 abstentions, rejected the discharge of the Secretary-General of the Council for the implementation of the 2023 budget, due to ongoing lack of cooperation with Parliament on the discharge procedure, hindering an informed decision and necessitating the refusal of discharge. The resolution emphasized the need for improved inter-institutional cooperation to address this issue.
Parliament proposes an overall budget of: The European Parliament approved a €193.9 billion budget, increasing the Commission's proposal by €597.8 million. The budget prioritizes maintaining funding for key programs like Erasmus+ and EU4Health amidst growing repayment costs for the recovery plan. Negotiations with EU governments commence in November.
Serbian leadership is politically respon: The European Parliament voted to hold Serbia's leadership accountable for escalating repression and weakening democracy, while supporting the right to peaceful protests and advocating for free and fair elections.
The European Parliament adopted by 515 v: The European Parliament approved a digital trade agreement with Singapore, passing it by 515 votes to 93, with 33 abstentions. The agreement establishes a modern framework for digital trade between the EU and Singapore, focusing on cross-border data flows, online consumer protection, and software source-code protection.
The European Parliament adopted by 572 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution (572 votes in favor, 18 against, 41 abstentions) for a digital trade agreement with Singapore, marking the EU's first stand-alone digital trade accord with an ASEAN member state. The agreement establishes binding rules for e-commerce trade.
€946,153,691 for Spain for the October 2: The European Parliament approved funding of €946.15 million for Spain to aid in October 2024 flood recovery, €89.63 million for Mayotte, France, following Cyclone Chido in December 2024, and €21.24 million for Réunion, France, after Cyclone Garance in February 2025.
The European Parliament adopted by 370 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to harmonize EU corporate tax frameworks for large cross-border companies (BEFIT), aiming to simplify business and investment. The proposal passed with 370 votes in favor, 160 against, and 107 abstentions.
The European Parliament adopted, by 454: The European Parliament passed an amendment to conserve and sustainably use marine biodiversity beyond national jurisdiction, with 454 votes in favor, 172 against, and 19 abstentions. The proposal aligns with the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea's BBNJ Agreement, and the directive's implementation will consider principles outlined in... (Referral back to the responsible committee for further negotiations.)
The European Parliament adopted by 543 v: The European Parliament passed, with 543 votes in favor and 72 against, a resolution to amend trade measures for Western Balkan countries and territories linked to the Stabilisation and Association Process. The Parliament approved the Commission's proposal, adopting it at first reading. The regulation, effective until December 31, 2025, exempts customs duties and equivalent charges for trade between the Union and these countries.
MEPs want to amend the EU Electoral Act: Parliament approved a proposal to grant MEPs proxy voting rights during pregnancy and post-childbirth, aiming to enhance gender equality and support mothers' political participation.
The European Parliament adopted by 490 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, emphasizing the significance of the EU-UK partnership, based on shared values and interests. The resolution underscores the importance of fully implementing the Withdrawal Agreement, Windsor Framework, and Trade and Cooperation Agreement.
The European Parliament adopted by 510 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 510 votes in favor, 75 against, and 80 abstentions, urging caution against military escalation in the Arctic, particularly Russia's and China's strategic activities, to maintain regional stability.
Persistent breaches of the rule of law t: MEPs voted to address threats to European values and the EU's legal order, expressing concern over judicial interference, corruption, misuse of EU funds, and attacks on civil society. They also called for tangible consequences if espionage allegations are proven and raised alarm about AI-generated political content ahead of the 2026 elections.
The European Parliament adopted by 426 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, with 426 votes in favor, 182 against, and 39 abstentions, addressing the growing use of AI in the EU's financial sector. The resolution emphasized the need for human oversight in the deployment of autonomous AI systems.
The European Parliament adopted by 400 v: The European Parliament passed an amendment to improve transparency in interest representation for third countries, with 400 votes in favor, 77 against, and 132 abstentions. Further negotiations are required.
The European Parliament adopted by 590 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to expedite the registration and use of biological control agents. The resolution calls for the EU Commission to establish a unified framework for approving these agents and products, with clear data requirements and updated guidelines, to boost EU innovation and promote sustainable alternatives.
The European Parliament adopted by 389 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution on the 2024 budget, focusing on the implementation of gender mainstreaming in the EU budget. The Commission's pilot methodology for tracking gender equality expenditure was welcomed, with the aim to improve gender equality in the budget process.
Next year’s EU budget signed into law by: The European Parliament has approved the 2026 EU budget, increasing funding for energy, transport infrastructure, humanitarian aid, and civil protection by over €372.7 million. The total budget stands at €192.8 billion in commitments, with payments set at €190.1 billion.
All businesses should have additional ye: The European Parliament voted to extend the compliance deadline for businesses by a year and to simplify due diligence requirements for certain operators and traders. A review of the EU's deforestation law is scheduled for April 2026.
The European Parliament adopted by 453 v: The European Parliament rejected, by a majority of 453 votes, the draft decision to allow genetically modified maize DAS1131 on the market, due to Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003. The decision was in response to an application by Corteva Agriscience Belgium B.V. for the use of this GM maize in foods, feed, and food ingredients.
The European Parliament adopted by 456 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 456 votes to 166, against renewing the authorization for genetically modified oilseed rape MON 88302 in products, excluding food and feed. The application for renewal, submitted by Bayer Agriculture B.V., was rejected.
The European Parliament adopted by 426 v: The European Parliament rejected, by a majority of 426 votes, the renewal of authorization for genetically modified maize MON 87427 in food and feed, following an application by Bayer Agriculture B.V. on March 19, 2024.
The European Parliament adopted by 453 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 453 votes to 179, against renewing the authorization for genetically modified soybean MON 87708 in products, as per Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003. The decision was in response to a 2024 application by Bayer Agriculture B.V. for renewal.
The European Parliament adopted by 519 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to adopt the Council's position on a draft amending budget for 2025, featuring an increase of €2 billion for the European Regional Development Fund (ERDF). The resolution passed with a majority of 519 votes, and the budget's overall impact on Member States' contributions was deemed minimal.

The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 519 votes in favor and 3 against, addressing the political crisis and humanitarian disaster of the Rohingya in Myanmar. The resolution, tabled by multiple groups, condemns the military coup and the resulting violence, including the arrest of nearly 30,000 people and the destruction of over 100,000 homes.
The European Parliament adopted by 464 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 464 votes in favor and 58 against, addressing the rule of law and human rights situation in Tunisia, specifically the case of lawyer and journalist Sonia Dahmani. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups, including EPP, S&D, Renew, Green/EFA, and the Left. Sonia Dahmani, arrested in May 2024, has faced multiple trials and convictions, with potential sentences totaling up to 24 years, and is reportedly subjected to inhumane detention conditions.
The European Parliament adopted by 539 v: The European Parliament unanimously passed a resolution, with 27 abstentions, condemning post-election killings and human rights deterioration in Tanzania, particularly the imprisonment of opposition leader Tundu Lissu. The resolution was tabled by multiple groups, including EPP, S&D, ECR, Renew, and Greens/EFA, following criticisms of the 2025 elections and subsequent violent crackdown by security forces.
The European Parliament adopted by 549 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by a large majority, condemning the escalating repression of the Baha'is in Iran, including arrests, property confiscation, and denial of rights. The resolution was tabled by multiple political groups.
The European Parliament adopted by 450 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 450 votes in favor and 93 against, to decarbonize and modernize EU fisheries, focusing on sustainable, socio-economically just, and inclusive transition by 2050. A realistic, scientifically-backed roadmap for decarbonization was emphasized.
The European Parliament adopted by 604 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 604 votes in favor and 37 against, to amend regulations establishing the European High Performance Computing Joint Undertaking. The amended text defines 'Artificial Intelligence Giga Factory' and 'AI Gigafactory Coordinator'.
The European Parliament adopted by 619 v: The European Parliament approved a regulation to adjust Brexit Adjustment Reserve funds for Member States, strengthening the 2024-2027 multiannual financial framework in response to new emergencies such as Russia's war against Ukraine, energy crisis, and inflation.
MEPs support unlocking funds under the E: Parliament approved releasing funds from the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund (EGF), aimed at aiding workers and companies impacted by globalisation or financial crises. The decision calls for immediate and efficient support for affected workers and businesses.
The European Parliament adopted by 585 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to provide up to €500 million in loans to Jordan for economic stability, passing by a large margin. This new macro-financial assistance aims to address Jordan's pressing economic challenges, such as high public debt.
Major EU funding programmes to be amende: The European Parliament voted to amend major funding programs to support defense projects, including Ukraine's defense industry, as part of the European Defence Fund. The amendment to Horizon Europe does not affect the outcome of budget negotiations.
The European Parliament adopted by 629 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 629 votes in favor and 17 against, to amend farming and rural development regulations, as well as data governance rules. The new rules ensure that arable land classifications remain unchanged by 2026.
The European Parliament adopted by 627 v: The European Parliament approved, with a large majority, an agreement to amend the scientific and technological cooperation accord between the EU and Jordan, allowing Jordan's participation in the Partnership for Research and Innovation in the Mediterranean Area (PRIMA). The Parliament consented to the agreement's conclusion.
Sustainability reporting will be require: Large corporations with over 1,000 employees and €450 million turnover must report sustainability, while smaller firms are protected. Due diligence rules for larger corporations (over 5,000 employees and €1.5 billion turnover) start in July 2029.
The European Parliament adopted by 417 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution to strengthen EU-Saudi Arabia relations, aiming for a strategic partnership in sectors like defense, culture, innovation, and energy. The 2026 EU-Gulf Cooperation Council summit in Riyadh is expected to further broaden ties and address mutual interests.
MEPs approve gradual ban on pipeline and: Parliament approves a phased ban on natural gas imports, starting from 2026. The Commission is tasked to prepare a ban on oil imports for 2027, with harmonized penalties for infringements.
All businesses will have one additional: The European Parliament voted to extend the compliance deadline for businesses by one year and simplified traceability requirements within the EU, while also easing due diligence requirements for small and micro primary operators, excluding printed products from the law's scope.
The European Parliament adopted by 477 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 477 votes in favor and 69 abstentions, condemning the arbitrary arrest and sentencing of academics Bahruz Samadov and Igbal Abilov in Azerbaijan. The resolution highlights the surge in political prisoners in Azerbaijan, with nearly 400 individuals detained on politically motivated charges since 2025. Both academics were sentenced to lengthy prison terms on charges of high treason.
The European Parliament adopted by 519 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, by 519 votes to 2, condemning the mass kidnapping of children from St Mary’s Catholic School in Papiri, Nigeria. The resolution followed a school attack on 21 November 2025, where 303 students and 12 teachers were abducted, amidst ongoing security issues in Nigeria.
The European Parliament adopted by 483 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution (483-68, 76 abstentions) addressing the impact of drones in modern warfare, emphasizing the need for EU adaptation to contemporary security challenges. The resolution highlights the widespread use of drones in conflicts globally, including Ukraine, Middle East, and Southeast Asia. It underscores the transformative effect of drone proliferation on modern battlefields, and the subsequent need for EU defense and resilience adjustments for its member states.
The European Parliament adopted by 318 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, with 318 votes in favor, 247 against, and 67 abstentions, to establish performance indicators for auditing and budgeting in support of future EU competitiveness. The resolution emphasizes the need for an EU budget framework to prioritize economic results and measurable policy outcomes, using SMART (specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, time-bound) indicators to ensure efficient, effective, and sustainable spending.
The European Parliament adopted by 565 v: The European Parliament approved, by a large majority, the agreement allowing the European Border and Coast Guard Agency to operate in Bosnia and Herzegovina. The consent to the agreement was given.
The European Parliament adopted by 440 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 440 votes in favor, 127 against, and 59 abstentions, to support the EU Global Human Rights Sanctions Regime (EU Magnitsky Act). The Act aims to combat impunity for human rights violations worldwide and strengthens the EU's human rights tools. Parliament also requested a comprehensive assessment of the EU Magnitsky Act's impact.
The European Parliament adopted by 492 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, with 492 votes in favor, 144 against, and 28 abstentions, advocating for a unified legal framework for innovative companies, named 'Societas Europaea Unificata' (S.EU, Unified European Company). The rules for this framework should be uniform across the EU, with no exceptions allowed by Member States.
The European Parliament adopted by 334 v: The European Parliament approved a resolution, 334-324, to seek the Court of Justice's opinion on the Treaty compatibility of the proposed partnership agreement between the EU and South American countries (Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay), and the interim trade agreement.
Parliament stands in full solidarity wit: The European Parliament unanimously supports Iran's protesters, calling for an end to executions, detainee releases, and accountability for regime crimes. They also advocate for strengthening EU sanctions and making EU relations dependent on democracy and human rights.
The European Parliament adopted by 582 v: The European Parliament unanimously passed a resolution, condemning the treatment of Joseph Figueira Martin, a humanitarian researcher, by the Wagner Group in the Central African Republic. Martin was kidnapped, tortured, and held in inhumane conditions without trial for over a year.
The European Parliament adopted by 503 v: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with 503 votes in favor and 100 abstentions, condemning the conviction and sentencing of pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai in Hong Kong. Lai, a British citizen, was found guilty of collusion with foreign forces and sedition, and has spent over 1800 days in solitary confinement since 2020.
